trajectory.note: Living room base — blown picture window onto green lawn; the load-bearing window-pull subject (interior)
Kitchen base — blown window above sink; clean-white whites to recover (interior)
Primary bedroom base — two tall blown windows; shadow lift + window-pull (interior)
Front exterior base — crooked verticals, over-green lawn, over-blue sky to calm (exterior)
Twilight exterior base — blue-hour; warm window glow; gentle grade (exterior)
Backyard base — over-green lawn + deep-blue sky to calm; straighten fence (exterior)
Agency house-look reference still — natural-HDR target the shared preset is chosen to match
NBP brokerage logo (1024px) — vectorized via connector for the rider-label artboard
Finalized CC uploads of the 6 merged-base scene JPEGs + the brokerage logo; returns presigned URLs that the per-scene grading chain and the logo vectorize step chain from.
Leveled horizons and corrected the leaning ~2-3deg verticals on all 6 scenes (brief: straight verticals / corrected perspective). Batched across the set so geometry is consistent.
Trimmed the ~3% crooked/empty wedge edges introduced by upright correction so every frame is a clean rectangle before grading.
Neutral exposure/contrast baseline across all 6 so the manual grade starts from a consistent point (brief: cohesive set).
Set the mid exposure between the bracket extremes — bright but realistic interiors (gentle gamma lift). Tool flags deprecation but the workflow specifies image_adjust_exposure; it completed cleanly.

Masked the blown daylight windows on the 3 window-bearing interiors (living/kitchen/bed) to isolate the over-exposed glazing for the window-pull. 3 connector calls; requestIds for s01/s02/s03 recorded.
Inverted each window mask to the room (non-window) region so the shadow lift opens interiors without touching the recovered windows. 3 calls (s01/s02/s03).
THE WINDOW-PULL: masked highlight recovery (-82) on each window region — pulls the clipped-white glazing down so exterior greenery/detail reads instead of blowing out. 3 calls (s01/s02/s03).

Masked shadow lift (+32) on the room region of each interior — opens dark corners while leaving the recovered windows intact (brief: open shadows, clean whites). 3 calls (s01/s02/s03). Exteriors skip this masked room step.
Global -22 on bright walls/ceilings and over-bright skies so interiors read evenly and exteriors do not clip (brief: bright but realistic). Batched across all 6.
Final overall punch: +6 brightness / +16 contrast for proper contrast without going over-edited. Batched across all 6.
Neutralized white balance toward clean whites (slight cool: a=4,b=-14) to kill mixed tungsten/daylight cast (brief: clean neutral whites). Batched across all 6.
Targeted HSL pass 1: calmed the over-green lawns and recovered window foliage (green -28 sat, +8 hue toward natural). Batched across all 6.
Targeted HSL pass 2: calmed over-blue/cyan skies (blue -22 sat) so exteriors read natural, not over-saturated. Second distinct color family on the same set — batched across all 6.
Gentle vibrance lift (+16 vibrance, -3 saturation) for lively-but-realistic color without oversaturating wood/skin. Batched across all 6.

Fetched exact Lightroom preset display names so the shared gallery look is applied by real name. Chose Color - Natural (minimal stylization, true-to-scene color) to match the agency house-look reference without over-editing.

Applied ONE shared gallery preset (Color - Natural) to every scene as the final unifying color pass — cohesive set across all 6 (brief: consistent look across the full gallery). 6 calls, same preset, distinct requestIds.

Exported PRINT-resolution sRGB JPEG (q7) of each of the 6 finished scenes at native master res 1444x962 — resize-only, upscale_factor 1 (no upscaling; honest to the ~1536px source cap). 6 connector calls.
Exported WEB/portal sRGB JPEG (q7) of each scene at 1200x799 long-edge — second derivative from the same graded master for MLS/portal upload. 6 connector calls.

Stage 1/3 of the client gallery proof: laid the white page, the Signage Red header band, brand wordmark, and the natural-HDR/window-pull/preset subhead. Multi-element layout = local (connector processes elements).
Stage 2/3: placed all 6 graded web JPEGs in a 3x2 framed grid with red index chips so the client sees the cohesive set at a glance.

Stage 3/3: set scene captions + the agent/listing footer read PROGRAMMATICALLY from listing_factsheet.csv (Eleanor Vance, phone, brokerage, 6 listings) and the tagline; saved the client proof PDF. PDF assembled locally (PIL) — the connector has no image->PDF tool.

Recommended a clean real-estate signage typeface pairing for the rider label headline+stats to inform the authored .ai template. Top tailored pick: Clother (Bold/Black) geometric sans. For the local render we use Helvetica Neue Condensed-Bold (address headline, matches brand Libre Franklin grotesque) + Helvetica Neue (stats/footer, body Inter feel).
Vectorized the NBP brokerage logo PNG to crisp editable SVG (sign-shop / .ai ready) — the linked-image logo Variable swapped into each rider artboard.

data-merge rendered locally from input_assets/listing_factsheet.csv (6 rows); the document_merge_data_* connector requires a human-authored template. One rider artboard per row (254x152mm + 3mm bleed @300dpi); all copy (status/address/beds/baths/sqft/price/agent_name/agent_phone/brokerage) read programmatically from the CSV, logo Variable swapped in from the connector-vectorized NBP mark.

data-merge rendered locally from listing_factsheet.csv (6 rows) — snapping row 2 (278 Evergreen Way) to show per-row Variable binding works across the set; the document_merge_data_* connector requires a human-authored template.

Rendered the merged rider labels to print PDF (6 artboards, bleed-preserving) for the sign shop. Rendered locally because the upstream merge is local (no human-authored .ai); the document_render_vector connector needs an .ai source. PDF assembled at 300dpi via PIL.

Rendered the same merged rider labels to PNG at print DPI for quick digital proofs / web preview — second format from the merge output. Per-row PNGs + a 2x3 proof contact sheet. Local render (merge is local; connector render_vector needs .ai).

Bundled the final deliverable set (6 print JPEGs + 6 web JPEGs + gallery proof PDF + 6 rider labels PDF/PNG + vector logo) into a client delivery index/cover with grade summary and listing table read from the CSV. Local packaging.

Asserted exact dims on all 24 raster outputs (6 print 1444x962, 6 web 1200x799, 6 rider PNG 3070x1865) + 3 PDFs nonzero + SVG. Visually re-read living/kitchen/twilight finals + rider 1/3/6 + proof + delivery index: window-pull held (no clipped whites, no HDR halo), clean neutral whites, calmed greens/skies, straight verticals, cohesive shared preset; rider copy correct per CSV; logo background keyed clean. No clipped text or collisions. PASS.

tasks.readme:
# Northern Bloom Properties — Weekly Just-Listed Stills Package (Task 6004)

Real-estate weekly listing stills: 6 scenes graded to a natural-HDR gallery look with masked
window-pull recovery, straightened verticals, clean neutral whites, calmed greens/skies, and ONE
shared gallery preset for a cohesive set — exported to print + web JPEGs — plus a batch of
Illustrator-style data-merged "just-listed" rider / yard-sign labels rendered to print PDF + PNG,
bundled into a client proof sheet.

All client copy (addresses, beds/baths/sqft, prices, agent name + phone, brokerage) was read
**programmatically** from `input_assets/listing_factsheet.csv` — never retyped.

## Asked deliverable → produced file(s)

| Asked (from task `deliverables`) | Produced | Dims / format | How it was made |
|---|---|---|---|
| 6 final natural-HDR scene stills (balanced exposure, recovered windows, opened shadows, clean whites, corrected verticals) | `print_jpeg/NBP_0{1..6}_*.jpg` | 1444×962 sRGB JPEG | Full per-scene connector grade chain (see below) |
| Straight verticals / corrected geometry on every frame | (baked into all 6) | — | `image_auto_straighten` (auto upright) → `image_crop_to_bounds` (trim wedge), all 6 batched |
| Masked window-pull on each window-bearing interior | (baked into scenes 01/02/03) | — | `image_select_by_prompt('bright windows')` → `image_adjust_highlights(-82)` masked; `image_invert_selection` → `image_adjust_dark_portions(+32)` masked room |
| A single shared gallery look across all 6 | (baked into all 6) | — | `image_list_presets` → `image_apply_preset("Color - Natural")` once per scene (6 calls, same preset) |
| Print-ready high-resolution JPEG of every scene | `print_jpeg/` (6 files) | 1444×962 sRGB JPEG q7 | `image_crop_and_resize` at native master res (resize-only, **no upscaling**) |
| Web/portal-optimized JPEG of every scene | `web_jpeg/` (6 files) | 1200×799 sRGB JPEG q7 | `image_crop_and_resize`, 2nd derivative from same master |
| Batch of just-listed rider / yard-sign labels (address, beds/baths/sqft, price, agent+phone) → print PDF + PNG | `NBP_rider_labels_print.pdf` + `rider_labels_png/rider_000{1..6}.png` + `NBP_rider_labels_proof.png` | 254×152 mm + 3 mm bleed @300 dpi (3070×1865 px) | Local data-merge (`compose_lib.data_merge`) — one artboard per CSV row |
| A local contact-sheet / gallery proof PDF of all 6 graded scenes | `NBP_gallery_proof.pdf` | 2200×1700 @200 dpi | Local PIL 3×2 branded contact sheet |
| (bonus) Brokerage logo vector | `NBP_logo_vector.svg` | SVG | `image_vectorize` (connector) on the NBP logo |
| (bonus) Delivery bundle index/cover | `NBP_delivery_index.pdf` | 1700×2200 @200 dpi | Local PIL cover, listing table from CSV |

## The per-scene grade chain (Adobe connector — 19 [C] op-types, run across all 6 scenes)

Upload (`asset_initialize/finalize_file_upload`, 7 files) → per-scene chain:
1. `image_auto_straighten` (level + upright, all 6) — corrects the ~2–3° leaning verticals.
2. `image_crop_to_bounds` (trim 3% wedge, all 6).
3. `image_apply_auto_tone` (neutral baseline, all 6).
4. `image_adjust_exposure` (exposure +0.35, gamma 1.15 — bright but realistic, all 6).
5. **Window-pull (interiors 01/02/03):** `image_select_by_prompt("bright windows")` → masked
   `image_adjust_highlights(-82)` pulls the clipped-white glazing down so exterior detail returns.
6. **Shadow lift (interiors):** `image_invert_selection` (→ room mask) → masked
   `image_adjust_dark_portions(+32)` opens dark corners, leaving recovered windows intact.
7. `image_adjust_light_portions(-22)` (tame bright walls/skies, all 6).
8. `image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast(+6 / +16)` (proper punch, all 6).
9. `image_adjust_color_temperature(a=4, b=-14)` (clean neutral whites, all 6).
10. `image_adjust_hsl` ×2: green −28 sat (calm over-green lawns) + blue −22 sat (calm over-blue skies), all 6.
11. `image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation(+16 vib / −3 sat)` (lively-but-realistic, all 6).
12. `image_list_presets` → `image_apply_preset("Color - Natural")` — the ONE shared cohesive look (6 calls).
13. `image_crop_and_resize` → print JPEG (×6) and web JPEG (×6).

Plus `font_recommend` (signage type pairing — top pick Clother Bold/Black) and `image_vectorize`
(logo → SVG). Every connector op's `requestId` is recorded in `../trajectory.json`.

## Honest limitations

- **Source resolution / "print" size.** The supplied merged-base scene JPEGs are 1536×1024; after
  upright correction + edge trim the graded masters are 1444×962. The print JPEGs are exported at this
  **native** resolution (resize-only, `upscale_factor: 1`) — they are **not** upscaled to a 3600 px /
  300 dpi 12 in master. At 300 dpi, 1444 px ≈ 4.8 in long edge (fine for a rider-card photo or small
  flyer); for a large print the sign shop would request true high-res RAW captures. We did **not**
  upscale and call it print-res.
- **Rider-label data-merge is LOCAL.** Rendered locally via `compose_lib.data_merge` from
  `listing_factsheet.csv` (6 rows). The Adobe `document_merge_data_vector` connector requires a
  human-authored `rider_label.ai` with genuine Illustrator Variables (unusable headless), so the merge
  and the PDF/PNG renders were produced locally — layout follows the authored-template spec exactly
  (red status banner top, address headline, 3-up bed/bath/sqft, price bottom-left, agent block
  bottom-right, logo top-right; 254×152 mm + 3 mm bleed). Actor labelled `local_datamerge` in the
  trajectory.
- **Logo background.** The supplied logo PNG had a solid white background (RGB, no alpha); we keyed
  near-white to transparent locally for clean placement on the white rider card. The connector
  `image_vectorize` SVG (`NBP_logo_vector.svg`) is the crisp sign-shop vector source.
- **PDFs assembled locally (PIL).** The connector has no image→PDF tool; the proof, rider, and index
  PDFs are assembled locally. Prepress PDF/X-4 + true bleed crop marks are a print-house step.
- **Window-pull is a tonal recovery, not generative.** Where the base window was fully clipped, the
  masked highlight pull recovers a soft readable gradient / faint greenery — it cannot invent exterior
  detail that was never captured. No HDR halos were introduced.

## Files
- `print_jpeg/` — 6 × print JPEG (1444×962)
- `web_jpeg/` — 6 × web/portal JPEG (1200×799)
- `rider_labels_png/` — 6 × rider label PNG (3070×1865, bleed)
- `NBP_rider_labels_print.pdf` — 6-page rider print PDF (bleed)
- `NBP_rider_labels_proof.png` — rider proof contact sheet
- `NBP_gallery_proof.pdf` — 6-scene client gallery proof
- `NBP_delivery_index.pdf` — delivery bundle cover/index
- `NBP_logo_vector.svg` — vectorized brokerage logo

input_assets.what_it_is: The 6-row data-merge source that drives the rider labels: status, address, beds, baths, sqft, price, agent_name, agent_phone, brokerage, logo — one row per just-listed property.

items.question: Does the CSV header row contain exactly the 10 columns in the exact order status,address,beds,baths,sqft,price,agent_name,agent_phone,brokerage,logo so they map 1:1 to the Illustrator Variables?
items.guidance: YES = header is precisely those 10 keys in that order. NO = any column missing, renamed, reordered, or extra columns present.
items.reference: Header is exactly: status,address,beds,baths,sqft,price,agent_name,agent_phone,brokerage,logo (10 columns, correct order). The JSON has the same keys in the same order under a single top-level 'rows' array. Correct.

items.question: Are there exactly 6 listing rows whose beds/baths/sqft/price scale consistently together (bigger sqft -> more beds and higher price)?
items.guidance: 5 = exactly 6 rows AND each step up in sqft pairs with equal-or-more beds and a higher price with no inversions. 1 = wrong row count or values that contradict each other (e.g. biggest sqft priced lowest).

items.question: Are status, agent_name, agent_phone, brokerage and logo identical on all 6 rows with the required literal values?
items.guidance: YES = every row has status='JUST LISTED', agent_name='Eleanor Vance', agent_phone='(555) 789-1234', brokerage='Northern Bloom Properties', logo='brokerage_logo.png'. NO = any row deviates (different agent, real-area code, brokerage spelled differently).
items.reference: All 6 rows: status 'JUST LISTED', agent 'Eleanor Vance', phone '(555) 789-1234', brokerage 'Northern Bloom Properties', logo 'brokerage_logo.png'. Phone uses the safe (555) area. Correct.

input_assets.what_it_is: Flat real-estate brokerage logo lockup (NBP monogram + roofline-keyhole mark + 'Northern Bloom Properties' wordmark) on a transparent background, used as the linked-image Variable on every rider label and vectorized for sign-shop print.

items.question: Does the logo read exactly 'NBP' as a monogram with 'Northern Bloom Properties' spelled correctly beneath, plus a small roofline/keyhole house mark?
items.guidance: YES = 'N','B','P' caps + correctly spelled 'Northern Bloom Properties' + the house/keyhole symbol, no garbled or extra letters. NO = any misspelling, missing word, or stray characters.
items.reference: Reads 'NBP' (N and P in signage red, B in ink black) beside a red roofline with a keyhole, 'Northern Bloom Properties' spelled correctly below. No stray text. Correct.

items.question: Is the logo flat 2-colour (signage red + ink black) with hard clean edges and no gradients/shadows, suitable for vectorization?
items.guidance: 5 = only red (~#C8102E) and near-black, flat fills, crisp edges. 3 = mostly flat but slight softness/anti-alias. 1 = gradients, drop shadows, extra colours, or photographic shading.
items.reference: Two flat colours (red #C8102E, ink black #1A1A1A), hard edges, no gradients/shadows. QC noted only subtle anti-aliasing. Clean enough to vectorize (it was, into NBP_logo_vector.svg). Score 4-5.

items.question: Is the logo delivered on a genuinely transparent background (checkerboard, no white card behind the mark)?
items.guidance: YES = transparent alpha, mark floats with no backdrop. NO = solid white/coloured card baked behind the logo.
items.reference: Displayed PNG shows the transparent checkerboard. (Note: sips reports hasAlpha=no / the outputs README says the supplied PNG was actually solid-white RGB and had to be keyed to transparency locally — so a strict checker may answer NO on the raw file. The visual intent is a transparent lockup.)

input_assets.what_it_is: A correctly-edited natural-HDR living-room still that defines the target gallery look (recovered window, neutral whites, straight verticals) which the editor matches the whole set to.

items.question: In the reference, is the large picture window already correctly recovered — exterior greenery readable through it rather than blown to pure white?
items.guidance: YES = soft, readable green hedge/foliage visible through the window, no HDR halo. NO = window is clipped white or has obvious halo fringing.
items.reference: The picture window clearly shows a green hedge/garden outside; whites are neutral, verticals plumb, no halos. This is the 'already correct' target, as intended.

items.question: Is the reference free of any text/watermark/logo and shot as a believable documentary interior (not CGI-glossy or over-saturated)?
items.guidance: YES = no letters/marks anywhere and a natural lived-in look. NO = any text/watermark, or an over-glossy/over-saturated render.
items.reference: No text, watermark or logo; natural neutral interior with fireplace, mirror, linen sofa. QC vision_score 10. Correct as a look target.

input_assets.what_it_is: Three interior merged-base RAW-style captures, each deliberately containing a fully blown daylight window and slightly leaning verticals so the masked window-pull and straighten steps are load-bearing.

items.question: Does each interior have at least one daylight window genuinely clipped to pure white with no exterior detail (so window-recovery is a real problem to solve)?
items.guidance: YES = the picture window (living), over-sink window (kitchen) and the two tall windows (bedroom) are all pure clipped white. NO = exterior detail already visible, making the window-pull redundant.
items.reference: Living: large picture window fully blown white. Kitchen: wide over-sink window blown white. Bedroom: both tall windows blown white. All present — window-pull is genuinely load-bearing.

items.question: Do the interiors show a believable handheld feel with verticals leaning ~2-3 degrees off-plumb (real perspective correction to do)?
items.guidance: 5 = clear, natural ~2-3 degree lean on door/wall/window edges across the set. 3 = barely perceptible tilt. 1 = perfectly plumb already (nothing to correct) or absurdly tilted.
items.reference: All three interiors lean slightly off-plumb (e.g. the living-room window/wall edges and bedroom window frames are subtly tilted), consistent with the brief's handheld brief. Score 4.

items.question: Do the interiors read as believable real-estate captures (lived-in, natural light, not CGI-glossy or over-saturated)?
items.guidance: 5 = convincingly photographic, natural materials and light. 1 = plasticky CGI, over-saturated, or HDR-haloed already.
items.reference: Living room (linen sofa, oak table, monstera), kitchen (white shaker cabinets, quartz island, stainless range), bedroom (linen bedding, oak nightstand) all read as natural documentary captures. QC scores 6-10 (kitchen dinged only for visible appliance branding).

input_assets.what_it_is: Three exterior merged-base captures: a daytime front facade and a backyard with deliberately over-green lawn / over-saturated blue sky to be calmed, plus a blue-hour twilight with warm window glow.

items.question: Do the daytime exteriors (front + backyard) show a deliberately over-saturated deep-blue sky and/or over-green lawn that an editor would need to calm down?
items.guidance: YES = punchy near-electric blue sky and vivid green grass beyond natural. NO = colours already restrained/neutral.
items.reference: Front exterior has a deep saturated blue sky and very green lawn; backyard has an intense blue sky and vivid green lawn. Both are over-cooked on purpose, giving the HSL calm-down step real work.

items.question: Is the twilight scene a believable blue-hour exterior with warm interior window glow and a deep dusk sky (not a flat daytime shot)?
items.guidance: 5 = convincing dusk sky gradient with warm glowing windows and porch light. 1 = looks like daytime or has an unnatural neon cast.
items.reference: Twilight shows a deep blue dusk sky with warm amber-lit windows and a lit covered patio — a convincing blue-hour real-estate hero. Score 5.

items.question: Are the exterior bases free of any text, watermark, logo, brand or recognizable trademark?
items.guidance: YES = no letters/marks/brands anywhere. NO = any visible signage, plate, or brand.
items.reference: No text/watermark/brand visible on front, twilight or backyard frames. Correct (QC pass on all three).

outputs.display: Graded interior scene stills — living / kitchen / bedroom (print + web JPEG)
outputs.what_it_is: The three graded interior masters exported as 1444x962 print JPEGs and 1200x799 web JPEGs, with straightened verticals, masked window-pull, opened shadows and clean neutral whites.

items.question: Has a masked window-pull visibly tamed the blown windows (highlights pulled, frame/edge detail returning) rather than leaving them identical to the base?
items.guidance: 5 = windows clearly recovered with readable exterior/gradient and intact frames, matching the reference. 3 = highlights softened and frame edges return but the glazing is still mostly bright. 1 = windows untouched / still pure clipped white exactly as the base.
items.reference: Window highlights were pulled and frame edges recovered (e.g. bedroom window mullions/sashes now read), but per the README the masked pull is tonal-only and cannot invent detail that was never captured — the living and bedroom glazing remains largely bright/white. Honest partial recovery: score ~3, NOT a full reference-grade greenery recovery.

items.question: Are the interiors brighter-but-realistic with opened shadows and clean neutral whites (no heavy colour cast, no over-edited HDR look)?
items.guidance: 5 = bright, even, neutral whites, shadow corners opened, no halos, no muddy cast. 1 = still dark/flat, or pushed into glowing over-HDR with colour cast.
items.reference: Graded interiors are noticeably brighter than the bases with opened shadows and neutral whites (auto-tone + exposure +0.35 + masked dark-portions +32 + colour-temp neutralize). No HDR halos introduced. Score 4.

items.question: Are the print JPEGs 1444x962 sRGB and the web JPEGs 1200x799 sRGB (resize-only, no upscaling)?
items.guidance: YES = print files measure 1444x962 and web files 1200x799, JPEG/sRGB. NO = wrong dimensions, wrong format, or visibly upscaled/soft.
items.reference: All print JPEGs measure 1444x962; all web JPEGs 1200x799. Native master res (resize-only, upscale_factor 1) per README. Correct, though note the README's honesty that these are not a true ~3600px/300dpi 12in print master.

outputs.display: Graded exterior scene stills — front / twilight / backyard (print + web JPEG)
outputs.what_it_is: The three graded exterior masters exported as 1444x962 print JPEGs and 1200x799 web JPEGs, with calmed greens/skies, straightened verticals and the same shared gallery preset.

items.question: Have the over-saturated blue skies and over-green lawns been visibly calmed toward natural while staying lively (not washed-out grey)?
items.guidance: 5 = sky and grass clearly less neon than the base yet still attractive and natural. 3 = partly calmed but still punchy. 1 = unchanged electric sky/grass, or over-corrected to dull grey.
items.reference: Front exterior: the deep electric-blue sky is calmed to a softer graduated blue and the lawn green is restrained vs the base, verticals corrected. Backyard: sky/grass toned down (still fairly contrasty). HSL green -28 / blue -22 applied. Front scores ~4; backyard a touch more aggressive.

items.question: Are the building/fence verticals on the exteriors plumb (the ~2-3 degree lean in the bases corrected) with the frame cleanly cropped (no straighten wedge)?
items.guidance: YES = house edges, porch posts and fence read vertical with no empty corner wedge from the rotation. NO = still leaning, or a transparent/black triangle left from the upright correction.
items.reference: Front facade edges and porch columns read vertical, backyard fence is upright, and the crop_to_bounds step trimmed the rotation wedge. Auto-straighten + crop_to_bounds applied to all 6. Correct.

items.question: Does the twilight scene keep its warm window glow and dusk mood after grading (the shared preset did not flatten it into a daytime look)?
items.guidance: 5 = warm glowing windows and deep dusk sky retained, contrast clean. 1 = glow killed, sky turned daytime-blue, or windows blown out by the brightening pass.
items.reference: Twilight master retains the amber window glow and deep blue dusk sky with the lit patio, only cleaned/balanced — the brightening chain did not wash out the blue hour. Score 4-5.

outputs.display: Cohesive shared gallery look across all 6 scenes
outputs.what_it_is: All 6 graded scenes viewed as a set, which must share one cohesive gallery 'look' (the single applied preset) so the listing reads as one consistent body of work.

items.question: Do all 6 graded scenes look like one cohesive gallery — consistent brightness, contrast, white balance and saturation character across interiors and exteriors?
items.guidance: 5 = the 6 feel unmistakably from the same edit/preset; tonality and colour are harmonious. 3 = mostly consistent but one or two frames drift. 1 = each scene clearly graded differently (mismatched warmth/contrast).
items.reference: One shared preset ('Color - Natural') was applied once per scene, on top of an identical grade chain; the gallery proof shows a harmonious natural set. Score 4-5.

items.question: Was a single shared preset applied across the whole set (not a different look per image)?
items.guidance: YES = the same one preset is used for all 6. NO = visibly different stylistic looks per scene, or an obviously different preset on some frames.
items.reference: README + trajectory: image_apply_preset('Color - Natural') called 6 times (same preset). One shared look. Correct.

outputs.display: Just-listed rider / yard-sign labels (6 PNGs + print PDF)
outputs.what_it_is: Six data-merged rider/yard-sign labels (one per CSV row) at 3070x1865 with 3mm bleed, rendered to PNG and a 6-page print PDF, following the authored rider_label.ai layout.

items.question: On each rider, do the address, beds, baths, sqft and price exactly match the corresponding CSV row (read from data, never retyped or transposed)?
items.guidance: YES = every field matches its row verbatim (e.g. rider_0001 = 1405 Willow Creek Rd, 2/1.5/1,450/$385,000; rider_0006 = 88 Goldenrod Path, 5/4/4,200/$1,280,000). NO = any wrong/transposed/garbled value.
items.reference: rider_0001: '1405 Willow Creek Rd, Northbrook', 2 BEDS / 1.5 BATHS / 1,450 SQ FT, $385,000. rider_0006: '88 Goldenrod Path, Winnetka', 5 / 4 / 4,200, $1,280,000. Both match the CSV exactly. Correct.

items.question: Does each rider follow the authored template layout: red 'JUST LISTED' banner top, bold address headline, centered 3-up beds/baths/sqft, price bottom-left, agent block bottom-right, NBP logo top-right?
items.guidance: 5 = all six zones present in the specified positions with correct hierarchy. 3 = present but one element mis-placed or weak. 1 = layout substantially wrong or elements missing.
items.reference: Each rider: red 'JUST LISTED' banner across the top, bold black address line, 3-up 'BEDS | BATHS | SQ FT' stat row with divider rules, 'LIST PRICE' + red price bottom-left, 'Eleanor Vance / (555) 789-1234 / Northern Bloom Properties' bottom-right, NBP logo top-right. Matches the authored spec. Score 5.

items.question: Do all 6 riders show the correct agent 'Eleanor Vance', phone '(555) 789-1234', brokerage 'Northern Bloom Properties' and the NBP logo, with 'Northern Bloom Properties' spelled correctly?
items.guidance: YES = agent/phone/brokerage and logo identical and correctly spelled on all 6. NO = any misspelling, wrong phone, or missing/incorrect logo.
items.reference: All 6 riders carry Eleanor Vance, (555) 789-1234, Northern Bloom Properties, and the NBP roofline logo top-right, spelling correct. Correct.

items.question: Are the rider PNGs 3070x1865 px (254x152mm + 3mm bleed at 300dpi) and is the print PDF a 6-page document?
items.guidance: YES = each PNG measures 3070x1865 and the PDF has one page per listing (6). NO = wrong pixel size or wrong page count.
items.reference: All six rider PNGs measure 3070x1865 (254x152mm + 3mm bleed @300dpi); NBP_rider_labels_print.pdf is the 6-page print PDF (~2.1MB). Correct. Note: produced via local data-merge, not the headless Illustrator connector (which needs the human-authored .ai).

outputs.what_it_is: A single 2000x1650 branded contact sheet laying out all 6 rider labels 3x2 for client sign-off.

tasks.title: Reality-show key-art rebuild — repaint every AI region of an approved competition-show poster with masked, licensed Adobe Stock photography, graded to one cinematic look, generative-expanded to 24x36 full-bleed and shipped as a genuine print-ready InDesign PDF plus a flattened web JPG
tasks.brief: Rebuild ONE network-approved AI key-art for the prime-time reality competition show “LAST ONE STANDING” so it reads as photographed, not generated. The composition and typography are LOCKED; the retoucher sources and licenses four real Adobe Stock plates (hero stand-in, foil stand-in, dusk sky, stadium-floor texture), normalizes/levels/cuts/depth-blurs each, then grades ALL to one warm-key cinematic look via a shared Lightroom preset and a matched 10-step relight, drops believable contact shadows, composites, generative-expands to a 24x36in full-bleed, re-vectorizes the title + billing-block lockup, and ships a genuine print-ready InDesign PDF (24x36 / 300dpi) plus a flattened web JPG with a stock-usage note. Provided inputs: the approved key-art (blend target), a dusk-arena mood/lighting reference, a print spec sheet, and a typography sample driving the logo rebuild.
tasks.mcp_workflow: asset_initialize_file_upload -> asset_finalize_file_upload -> asset_search (hero) -> asset_license_and_download_stock -> asset_search (foil+sky+environment) -> asset_license_and_download_stock -> image_apply_auto_tone (per plate) -> image_auto_straighten -> image_crop_to_bounds -> image_select_subject -> image_remove_background (hero) -> image_select_subject -> image_remove_background (foil) -> image_select_by_prompt (sky) -> image_invert_selection -> image_apply_gaussian_blur (blurTarget:background) -> image_apply_lens_blur (sky) -> image_adjust_exposure -> image_adjust_highlights -> image_adjust_light_portions -> image_adjust_dark_portions -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast -> image_adjust_color_temperature -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> image_adjust_single_color_saturation -> image_adjust_hsl -> image_list_presets -> image_apply_preset (all plates) -> image_apply_color_overlay -> image_fill_area (contact shadows) -> font_recommend -> image_vectorize (logo lockup) -> document_render_vector -> image_generative_expand (24x36 bleed) -> image_crop_and_resize (24x36@300dpi + web JPG) -> PIL local compose -> document_convert_pdf -> document_render_layout (print-ready 24x36 300dpi PDF)

trajectory.note: Network-approved AI key-art (LOCKED comp + typography) — the blend/match target the rebuilt photographic plates must hit.
Dusk-arena mood/lighting reference — single warm key the final blend is checked against (drives exposure/temperature targets).
Locked typography specimen — title + tagline + billing block re-vectorized for the print lockup; source of all on-poster copy.
Pushed the 3 client inputs (approved key-art, mood ref, typography specimen) into Adobe storage; presigned URLs feed the whole chain.
Searched Adobe Stock against the approved comp and licensed the HERO stand-in (full-body sportsman, clean bg) to replace the lead AI figure.
Searched + licensed the 3 remaining plates — FOIL stand-in, dusk-gradient SKY, empty stadium ENVIRONMENT — matching the AI background regions.
Normalized all 4 licensed plates to a neutral tonal baseline so the shared grade math starts from one consistent point.
Leveled the empty-stadium plate so the floor/horizon are true before it is cut and composited under the cast.
Trimmed 3% rotation slack off every edge of the straightened arena plate, locking clean edges for the composite.
Auto-selected the HERO stand-in and stripped its background to a clean alpha cut-out that drops over the AI hero region.
Auto-selected the FOIL stand-in and stripped its background to a second clean alpha cut-out for the rival region.
Isolated the upper stands/sky region of the arena plate, then inverted it to a foreground floor-region mask so depth can be applied separately.
Background-target gaussian blur on the arena plate so the deep stadium recedes while the floor under the cast stays readable.
Whole-plate lens blur on the standalone dusk sky for an optically believable far-distance bokeh fall-off behind everything.
First four of the matched relight on the background plate: lift exposure to the dusk mood, pull floodlight highlights, knock down bright stands, deepen the floor shadows where the cast will sit.
Set the punchy key-art contrast, then the big warm-key temperature push toward the single amber light the poster is lit with.
Calmed overall saturation, reined in the over-blue floodlight channel, and shifted the residual blue hue warm so the arena reads under one warm key, not stadium cyan.
Listed the 110+ Lightroom presets to choose one shared cinematic look applied identically across all four plates.
Applied ONE shared cinematic split-tone (teal shadows / warm highlights) to env, sky, hero and foil so every layer carries identical tonal DNA — one lit set, no AI-look mismatch.
Washed the amber key colour (#E8A24A) across the background on softLight to marry the plates under one lighting colour, selling the single warm-key dusk read.

image_apply_preset flattened the cast cut-out transparency; transplanted each original cut-out alpha back onto its graded RGB (grade is non-spatial, geometry identical) to restore clean RGBA cast plates for compositing.
Confirmed a bold condensed display + high-visibility poster typeface pairing for the title + billing-block lockup, matched to the locked Archivo-style comp.
Vectorized the title + billing-block lockup from the locked typography specimen into a crisp scalable SVG for print, replacing the soft AI raster logo.

document_render_vector rejects SVG (needs PDF/PostScript .ai); rendered the genuine connector-produced vector SVG to a 2048px print proof locally via macOS Quick Look so it can be placed in the layout.
Selected the foreground floor strip and multiply-filled it with deep arena-shadow tone (#1C2230, 38%) to seat believable contact-shadow density under where the cast cut-outs anchor.
Stage 1: laid the graded dusk-sky plate over the graded+contact-shadowed stadium and added the warm ember horizon glow, matching the approved comp geometry.
Stage 2: dropped soft elliptical contact shadows on the field where the two cast cut-outs anchor, so the figures sit on the ground not float.

Stage 3: placed the hero (left, forward) and foil (right, back) as rim-lit dark silhouettes with warm key-left / cool steel-right rims, then a global warm wash + vignette to marry them as one lit set.
Generative-expanded the composed 2:3 key-art outward on all sides so there is real outpainted arena/sky image data into the trim/bleed margin for the 24x36 full-bleed print.
Locked the generative-expanded canvas to the exact 24x36 (2:3) print aspect; this connector-trimmed master feeds the local print/web assembly.

Stage 3 layout: assembled the connector scene master + locked title lockup, amber tagline, premiere line, network bug and billing block into the final 24x36 poster (4800x7200 trim); all copy read from copy.json.
Converted the flattened 24x36 composite PDF into a genuine InDesign document (.indd, returned as a package ZIP) as the print-layout container.
Rendered the InDesign layout to the final print-ready PDF at 300dpi — the primary print deliverable alongside the flattened web JPG.

Asserted all output dimensions (print master 4800x7200, bleed 4850x7250, web 1200x1800, trim aspect exactly 1.5), confirmed the InDesign PDF is a valid %PDF with MediaBox + the vector SVG, and visually read the title band + InDesign PDF + bleed corner — copy crisp, correctly spelled, no clipping/collisions, palette correct.

tasks.readme:
# LAST ONE STANDING — Reality-show key-art rebuild (Task 1847)

**Client:** VANTAGE NETWORK (broadcast / reality competition) · **Brief:** rebuild the network-approved
AI key-art so it reads as *photographed*, using licensed Adobe Stock plates, one cinematic grade,
generative-expanded to a 24×36 full-bleed, shipped as a genuine InDesign print PDF + a flattened web JPG.

The composition + typography were **locked**; every AI region was re-sourced from real licensed Adobe
Stock and graded to one warm-key dusk look. All on-poster copy was read programmatically from the locked
typography specimen (`work/copy.json`) — nothing retyped by hand.

## Asked deliverable → produced file → how it was made

| Asked deliverable | Produced file | px | How |
|---|---|---|---|
| Licensed Stock matching every AI region + usage note | `STOCK_USAGE_NOTE.md` | — | 4 plates licensed live: hero 115155591, foil 473425796, sky 654020866, env 239375308 (`asset_search` → `asset_license_and_download_stock`) |
| Each plate normalized / leveled / subject-cut | (intermediates in `work/`) | — | `image_apply_auto_tone` (4 plates) → `image_auto_straighten` + `image_crop_to_bounds` (env) → `image_select_subject` + `image_remove_background` (hero, foil) |
| Background + sky depth-blurred | (intermediates) | — | `image_select_by_prompt`(sky) → `image_invert_selection` → `image_apply_gaussian_blur` (blurTarget background) on env; whole-plate `image_apply_lens_blur` on the standalone sky |
| Every layer re-lit to ONE warm key + shared preset | (intermediates) | — | 9-step matched relight (exposure→highlights→light/dark portions→brightness/contrast→temperature→vibrance→single-colour blue→HSL) + ONE shared `image_apply_preset` "Creative - Cool Shadows & Warm Highlights" across all 4 plates |
| Contact shadows + warm key wash married | (intermediates) | — | `image_apply_color_overlay` (#E8A24A softLight key wash) + `image_select_by_prompt`(floor) → `image_fill_area` (#1C2230 multiply) for contact density; per-figure elliptical contact shadows painted locally |
| Title + billing-block lockup re-vectorized + proofed | `LAST_ONE_STANDING_logo_lockup_vector.svg`, `…_logo_lockup_proof.png` | 2048² proof | `image_vectorize` on the locked specimen → SVG; print proof rasterized locally (render_vector needs a PDF/.ai, not SVG) |
| Canvas generative-expanded to 24×36 full bleed | (intermediate `work/scene_expanded.png`) | 2660×3860 | `image_generative_expand` (130px each side, seed 1847) → real outpainted arena/sky into the bleed → `image_crop_and_resize` locked the exact 2:3 |
| **Print-ready InDesign PDF (24×36 / 300dpi)** | **`LAST_ONE_STANDING_24x36_InDesign_print.pdf`** | 24×36 page | flattened composite PDF → `document_convert_pdf` → genuine `.indd` → `document_render_layout` @300dpi |
| **Flattened web JPG** | **`LAST_ONE_STANDING_web_1200x1800.jpg`** | 1200×1800 | sRGB downscale of the trim master (per `print_spec` web_jpg_size) |
| Print master (trim) + prepress bleed/crop-marks | `…_24x36_print_master.jpg`, `…_24x36_bleed_cropmarks.jpg` | 4800×7200 / 4850×7250 | local layout assembly of the connector scene master + locked typography |

## Connector ops (25 distinct tools, ~37 calls)
asset_initialize/finalize_file_upload · asset_inline_preview · asset_search ×5 (StockAsset) ·
asset_license_and_download_stock ×4 · image_apply_auto_tone · image_auto_straighten ·
image_crop_to_bounds · image_select_subject ×2 · image_remove_background ×2 · image_select_by_prompt ×3 ·
image_invert_selection · image_apply_gaussian_blur · image_apply_lens_blur · image_adjust_exposure ·
image_adjust_highlights · image_adjust_light_portions · image_adjust_dark_portions ·
image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast · image_adjust_color_temperature ·
image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation · image_adjust_single_color_saturation · image_adjust_hsl ·
image_list_presets · image_apply_preset ×4 · image_apply_color_overlay · image_fill_area ·
font_recommend · image_vectorize · image_generative_expand · image_crop_and_resize ·
document_convert_pdf · document_render_layout.

## Honest limitations
- **Print resolution.** Licensed Stock plates are 3.3k–6k px on their long edge; the composited photographic
  scene is ~2.6k px. The 4800×7200 layout master is built at **24×36 / 200 dpi**: the re-vectorized
  type/lockup render crisp at full res, but the *photographic* plate is scaled to fit, not native 300 dpi.
  True 24×36 @ 300 dpi print is a print-house upsample from this master (no upscaling was passed off as
  native print-res here, per the contract).
- **InDesign PDF page size.** `document_convert_pdf` inherited the source PDF page geometry (2:3, 24×36
  proportion). The rendered `.indd` PDF carries the full poster; a prepress operator sets the exact
  24×36 trim box + SWOP CMYK conversion at the print house (the spec is in `input_assets/print_spec.json`).
- **Logo proof render.** `document_render_vector` requires a PDF/PostScript `.ai`; the `image_vectorize`
  output is SVG, so the print proof PNG was rasterized locally from that genuine connector SVG.
- **Cast = silhouettes.** The locked comp shows the cast as rim-lit dark silhouettes; the licensed
  full-colour stand-ins were cut, graded, then rendered as two-rim silhouettes (warm key left / steel
  rim right) locally to match — the faces are intentionally not visible, exactly as in the approved comp.
- **Contact shadow + final multi-element layout** are local (`local_compositor`) — the connector processes
  elements but cannot headlessly composite a hand-painted shadow or arbitrary multi-element layout.

## Files
- `LAST_ONE_STANDING_24x36_InDesign_print.pdf` — primary print deliverable (genuine .indd render)
- `LAST_ONE_STANDING_24x36_print_master.jpg` — 4800×7200 trim master
- `LAST_ONE_STANDING_24x36_bleed_cropmarks.jpg` — 4850×7250 prepress (bleed + crop marks)
- `LAST_ONE_STANDING_web_1200x1800.jpg` — flattened sRGB web JPG
- `LAST_ONE_STANDING_logo_lockup_vector.svg` / `…_proof.png` — re-vectorized title+billing lockup
- `STOCK_USAGE_NOTE.md` — every licensed Adobe Stock asset id + license

input_assets.what_it_is: The network-approved AI-generated LAST ONE STANDING poster (1024x1536) whose composition, colour and typography are LOCKED and which the rebuild must match.

items.question: Does the comp show the locked composition: a confident HERO figure on the LEFT (slightly forward) and a FOIL/rival on the RIGHT, both as rim-lit silhouettes at a dusk stadium arena with an amber-to-blue dusk sky?
items.guidance: 5 = two opposed silhouetted figures (left hero in tactical vest/cap, right rival in plain tee) face off on an arena floor under a warm dusk sky with the empty stadium bowl receding behind. 1 = only one figure, no arena, or daylight/non-dusk scene.
items.reference: Two dark silhouetted male figures face each other on a stadium floor; left figure (vest + ball-cap) slightly forward, right figure (plain tee) slightly back; amber-orange sunset band low on the horizon graduating to deep blue sky; empty stadium seating bowl behind. Matches the locked composition.

items.question: Are all four locked copy elements present and legibly spelled on the comp: title 'LAST ONE STANDING', tagline 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.', premiere line 'SERIES PREMIERE THURSDAY 9/8c', and a network bug top-left?
items.guidance: Yes = the heavy condensed cream title 'LAST ONE STANDING' spans the lower third, 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.' sits beneath it, 'SERIES PREMIERE THURSDAY 9/8c' runs along the very bottom, and a network bug sits top-left. No = any of these strings is missing or misspelled.
items.reference: Title 'LAST ONE STANDING' (cream, heavy condensed) across lower third; 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.' directly beneath; 'SERIES PREMIERE THURSDAY 9/8c' at the very bottom (THURSDAY 9/8c in amber). All four present and correctly spelled.

items.question: Does the top-left network bug in this approved comp read 'ANTAGE NETWORK' (the leading 'V' of VANTAGE is cut off / dropped) rather than the full 'VANTAGE NETWORK'?
items.guidance: Yes = the wordmark visibly starts with 'ANTAGE' because the V is clipped at the left edge / missing, with a small beetle-style bug icon above it. No = the full 'VANTAGE NETWORK' is rendered. (This is a known defect in the AI comp the rebuild is expected to correct to the full word.)
items.reference: The bug reads 'ANTAGE NETWORK' with the leading V dropped (confirmed in manifest QC issues), accompanied by a small beetle/bug glyph top-left. This is the AI defect the rebuilt poster should fix to 'VANTAGE NETWORK'.

items.question: Does the comp read as a slightly synthetic / AI-generated image (the 'AI look' the retoucher is being hired to rebuild with real photography)?
items.guidance: 5 = silhouettes and floor look a touch plasticky/CG-rendered and the warm key is uneven (cooler floor/right figure), i.e. clearly a comp needing a photographic rebuild. 1 = already indistinguishable from a real photograph, defeating the task premise.
items.reference: Figures are clean CG-style silhouettes, the arena floor is darker/cooler than the warm-key figures, and the overall finish is glossy-synthetic — exactly the AI look the brief says the retoucher must rebuild with licensed Stock.

input_assets.what_it_is: A photographic mood/lighting reference frame (1024x1536) of one warmly-lit athlete in a dusk arena that the final blend's lighting must be checked against.

items.question: Does the reference establish the target lighting: a single warm key from camera-left on one real-looking athlete at a blue-hour dusk arena, with an ember band on the horizon?
items.guidance: 5 = one believable person lit by a single warm amber key from the left, cool falloff on the far side, a glowing ember/orange band low on the horizon and an out-of-focus stadium bowl behind. 1 = flat/multi-source studio light, daylight, or no arena.
items.reference: A woman in a grey athletic tank stands at a dusk stadium; a warm amber key rakes her from camera-left, the horizon glows ember-orange under a darkening sky, and the empty stadium seating recedes softly out of focus behind her.

items.question: Does the reference read as a genuine candid photograph (natural skin texture, real hair with flyaways, believable sweat sheen) rather than CGI-glossy or over-retouched?
items.guidance: 5 = visible skin pores, individual hair strands and flyaways, slight sweat sheen and fabric wrinkles, shallow real depth of field. 1 = plastic/painted skin, helmet hair, CGI gloss or heavy beauty retouching.
items.reference: Realistic 85mm-style frame: natural skin texture with sweat sheen on the shoulder/chest, loose hair strands escaping the bun, soft real bokeh on the stadium behind — reads as a real photo, not CGI (manifest vision_score 8).

items.question: Is the mood reference free of any text, letters, numbers, logos or watermarks (as the brief requires for a pure lighting reference)?
items.guidance: Yes = the frame contains no typography or marks of any kind. No = any letter, number, logo or watermark appears anywhere.
items.reference: The frame is purely photographic with zero text, numbers, logos or watermarks anywhere — it is a clean lighting reference only.

input_assets.what_it_is: A flat charcoal-on-white typography specimen (1024x1536) of the title lockup, tagline and full billing block, used to drive font_recommend and the re-vectorized logo.

items.question: Does the specimen contain, vertically stacked: the title 'LAST ONE STANDING', the tagline 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.', and a small-caps billing block including 'VANTAGE NETWORK PRESENTS', 'HOSTED BY DELIA VANCE' and 'CREATED BY THE MERIDIAN COLLECTIVE'?
items.guidance: Yes = title lockup at top (with a small standing-figure silhouette mark), tagline beneath, then the multi-line billing block with the studio, host and credits. No = any of these blocks missing.
items.reference: Top: heavy condensed title 'LAST ONE STANDING' with an amber standing-figure silhouette mark to the right; tagline 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.'; billing block: 'VANTAGE NETWORK PRESENTS / A MERIDIAN UNSCRIPTED PRODUCTION / “LAST ONE STANDING” / HOSTED BY DELIA VANCE / EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS RAY OKONKWO PRIYA NAIR / CREATED BY THE MERIDIAN COLLECTIVE'.

items.question: On the title line, does the first letter of 'LAST' render as a degraded glyph (a dot/period, reading '.AST ONE') rather than a clean capital 'L'?
items.guidance: Yes = the L of LAST is reduced to a small dot/stub so the line reads '.AST ONE STANDING'. No = a clean full-height capital L. (This specimen defect is what later propagates into the vectorized logo proof.)
items.reference: The leading 'L' of LAST is rendered as a small dot/period, so the title reads '.AST ONE STANDING' on the first line — a specimen flaw that carries through to the vectorized lockup proof.

items.question: Is the specimen a clean, high-contrast, vectorizable reference (solid charcoal type on a plain white background, no photo scene, no gradients/glow)?
items.guidance: 5 = flat charcoal-black letterforms isolated on pure white, one amber accent allowed on the title mark, crisp edges ready for image_vectorize. 1 = photographic background, gradients, glow or low contrast that would vectorize poorly.
items.reference: Flat charcoal type on plain white with a single amber silhouette mark on the title; no photographic background, no gradients or glow — a crisp specimen suitable for font_recommend + image_vectorize (manifest vision_score 10).

input_assets.what_it_is: The fixed 12-row print spec (24x36 in, 300dpi, CMYK SWOP, bleed/safe margins, web JPG size) supplied as a JSON object and a rendered CSV.

items.question: Does the spec list the correct fixed print numbers: trim 24x36 in, 300 dpi, trim 7200x10800 px, 0.125 in bleed, full-bleed 7275x10875 px, CMYK U.S. Web Coated (SWOP) v2, and web JPG 1200x1800?
items.guidance: Yes = all of those param/value pairs appear and are internally consistent (e.g. 24in x 300dpi = 7200px; 7200+2*37.5 = 7275). No = any value wrong, missing, or inconsistent.
items.reference: trim_width 24in, trim_height 36in, resolution 300dpi, trim_px 7200x10800, bleed 0.125in (37.5px/edge), full_bleed_px 7275x10875, color_mode CMYK SWOP v2, web_jpg_size 1200x1800 — all 12 rows present and arithmetically consistent.

items.question: Do the JSON and CSV carry the same 12 rows with identical param/value/unit/note columns?
items.guidance: Yes = the CSV header is exactly param,value,unit,note and every JSON spec row appears as a matching CSV line. No = rows differ, are missing, or columns are renamed.
items.reference: Both files hold the same 12 rows; CSV header is 'param,value,unit,note' and each row (trim_width...file_outputs) matches the JSON spec objects one-to-one.

outputs.what_it_is: The flattened sRGB web deliverable: the rebuilt LAST ONE STANDING poster downscaled to 1200x1800 (2:3).

items.question: Is the web JPG exactly 1200x1800 px (2:3) as specified in print_spec web_jpg_size?
items.guidance: Yes = pixel dimensions are 1200 wide x 1800 tall. No = any other size or aspect ratio.
items.reference: File is exactly 1200x1800 px, matching print_spec row web_jpg_size (1200 x 1800, flattened sRGB web JPG).

items.question: Does the web poster carry all locked copy correctly: title 'LAST ONE STANDING', tagline 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.', 'SERIES PREMIERE THURSDAY 9/8c', a top-left 'VANTAGE NETWORK' bug, and the 6-line billing block at the bottom?
items.guidance: Yes = every string is present and correctly spelled, including the full 'VANTAGE NETWORK' (V restored) and the billing block (VANTAGE NETWORK PRESENTS ... CREATED BY THE MERIDIAN COLLECTIVE). No = any string missing or misspelled.
items.reference: Title 'LAST ONE STANDING' (two cream lines over the floor), tagline 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.' in amber, 'SERIES PREMIERE THURSDAY 9/8c' below, 'VANTAGE NETWORK' bug top-left with the V restored, and the full 6-line billing block at the bottom — all present and matching work/copy.json.

items.question: Has the AI comp been rebuilt to read as a photographic composite: a real licensed dusk sky with clouds, a real (blurred) stadium environment behind the figures, and one cohesive warm-key cinematic grade?
items.guidance: 5 = the sky is a believable photographed twilight cloudscape, the stadium bowl behind reads as a real depth-blurred photo, and all elements share one warm-amber/cool-blue dusk grade. 1 = still the flat CG comp, mismatched grades, or obviously fake sky/environment.
items.reference: Real Adobe Stock sky plate (id 654020866, orange-gold twilight clouds) and stadium plate (id 239375308, lit empty stadium) composited behind the two figures with a shared warm-highlight/cool-shadow grade — a clear photographic rebuild of the AI comp.

items.question: Are the two cast figures rendered as deliberate rim-lit silhouettes (hero left/larger, rival right/smaller) consistent with the locked comp, rather than as broken cut-outs with halos or hard matte edges?
items.guidance: 5 = two clean dark silhouettes with subtle warm/steel rim light, left figure larger and forward, right smaller and back, edges clean against the sky/stadium. 1 = visible cut-out halos, hard jagged mattes, or wrong figure positions.
items.reference: Two solid dark silhouettes: a larger hero on the left (slightly forward, hands at hips) and a smaller rival on the right (back), each with a faint warm/steel rim — matching the locked comp's silhouette treatment (per README, intentional).

outputs.what_it_is: The high-res trim master (4800x7200) and a prepress version (4850x7250) with added bleed and crop marks.

items.question: Is the trim master 4800x7200 px (exact 2:3, the 24x36 proportion) and the bleed version larger by a consistent bleed margin (4850x7250) with crop marks?
items.guidance: Yes = master is 4800x7200 (2:3) and the bleed file is 4850x7250 with visible crop/registration marks at the corners. No = wrong dimensions, non-2:3 ratio, or no crop marks on the bleed file.
items.reference: print_master is 4800x7200 (2:3 = 24x36); bleed_cropmarks is 4850x7250 (+25px each edge) with corner crop marks. README discloses this master is built at 24x36/200dpi, not native 300dpi.

items.question: Does the print master reproduce the same composition and copy as the web JPG at higher resolution (same sky, figures, title, tagline, premiere line and billing block)?
items.guidance: 5 = identical layout and copy to the web version, just larger and with the billing block legible. 1 = different composition, missing elements, or copy that disagrees with the web JPG.
items.reference: The 4800x7200 master shows the same dusk-sky + stadium + two silhouettes + 'LAST ONE STANDING' title, 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.' tagline, 'SERIES PREMIERE THURSDAY 9/8c' and billing block as the web JPG, at higher resolution.

items.question: Does the prepress bleed version correctly extend imagery into the bleed (no white edges) while keeping title and billing block inside the safe area?
items.guidance: 5 = the sky/stadium imagery bleeds fully to the trimmed edge with crop marks outside the trim, and the title + billing block sit comfortably inside the 0.25in safe margin. 1 = white/empty bleed, type clipped by the trim, or marks placed inside the live area.
items.reference: Imagery fills to the bleed edge with corner crop marks beyond the trim; title and 6-line billing block remain inset from the edges within the safe area per print_spec (safe_margin 0.25in).

outputs.what_it_is: The primary print deliverable: a genuine InDesign-produced PDF carrying the full LAST ONE STANDING poster.

items.question: Is the PDF a genuine Adobe InDesign render (Creator = Adobe InDesign, Producer = Adobe PDF Library) rather than a generic PIL/print-to-PDF export?
items.guidance: Yes = PDF metadata shows Creator 'Adobe InDesign' and Producer 'Adobe PDF Library'. No = generic producer (e.g. PIL, Cairo, browser print).
items.reference: PDF metadata: Creator 'Adobe InDesign 21.3 (Windows)', Producer 'Adobe PDF Library 18.0' — a genuine InDesign render via document_convert_pdf -> document_render_layout, as the brief demands.

items.question: Is it a single-page PDF that embeds the full poster image (the rebuilt LAST ONE STANDING composition)?
items.guidance: Yes = one page containing the embedded poster raster with the figures, sky, title and billing block. No = blank page, multiple unrelated pages, or no embedded image.
items.reference: Single-page PDF with an embedded /Image XObject carrying the full rebuilt poster (figures, dusk sky, title, tagline, premiere line, billing block).

items.question: Is the page geometry a clean 2:3 portrait matching the 24x36 proportion, with the README honestly disclosing that the literal 24x36 trim box + SWOP CMYK conversion is set by the print house?
items.guidance: 5 = MediaBox is a clean 2:3 portrait (e.g. 1152x1728 pt) AND the README transparently states the exact 24x36 trim box and CMYK SWOP conversion happen at prepress. 1 = wrong/landscape geometry or an undisclosed claim that the PDF is already final 24x36 CMYK.
items.reference: MediaBox 1152x1728 pt (= 16x24in, a clean 2:3 = 24x36 proportion); README 'Honest limitations' discloses the exact 24x36 trim box and SWOP CMYK conversion are applied by the prepress operator. Honest, not overclaimed.

outputs.display: Re-vectorized title + billing lockup (SVG + proof PNG)
outputs.what_it_is: The title/billing-block lockup re-vectorized from the locked specimen as an SVG plus a 2048x2048 rasterized print proof.

items.question: Is the SVG a genuine multi-path vector (produced by image_vectorize) rather than just an embedded bitmap wrapped in SVG?
items.guidance: Yes = the SVG contains many independent <path> elements describing the letterforms. No = a single <image> tag embedding a raster.
items.reference: SVG is 1024x1536 with ~310 distinct <path> elements describing the letterforms and silhouette mark — a real vectorization from image_vectorize, not an embedded bitmap.

items.question: Does the vectorized proof reproduce the source specimen's defect where the first 'L' of LAST renders as a dot, reading '.AST ONE STANDING'?
items.guidance: Yes = the proof title reads '.AST ONE STANDING' (leading L is a dot) inherited from the specimen. No = the proof shows a clean 'LAST ONE STANDING'. (Flag this as a defect carried through the pipeline that the rebuild should have corrected.)
items.reference: The proof PNG title reads '.AST ONE STANDING' with the leading L as a dot, an artifact inherited from the typography_sample input ('.AST ONE'). The rebuilt poster's own title is correct, but the vectorized lockup proof carries the flaw.

items.question: Is the proof a clean high-res raster (2048x2048) that includes the title lockup, the amber standing-figure silhouette mark and the start of the billing block?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp 2048x2048 proof showing the heavy condensed title, the amber figure mark beside 'ONE', the tagline and the top billing lines, all sharp. 1 = low-res/blurry, missing the figure mark, or missing the billing block.
items.reference: 2048x2048 proof: heavy condensed title with the amber standing-figure silhouette mark to the right of 'ONE', tagline 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.', and billing lines beginning 'VANTAGE NETWORK PRESENTS / A MERIDIAN UNSCRIPTED PRODUCTION'.

outputs.what_it_is: The required stock-usage note listing every licensed Adobe Stock asset id, title, resolution and license type used to rebuild each AI region, plus the build README.

items.question: Does the usage note list exactly four licensed Adobe Stock plates with asset ids, mapped one-to-one to the four AI regions (hero, foil, dusk sky, stadium-floor/environment)?
items.guidance: Yes = four rows: hero stand-in, foil stand-in, dusk-gradient sky, stadium-floor/environment, each with a distinct asset id and title. No = fewer than four, missing ids, or wrong region mapping.
items.reference: Four plates: HERO 115155591 (sportsman, 3604x5406), FOIL 473425796 (young man in white tee, 3333x5000), SKY 654020866 (orange-gold twilight, 6000x4000), ENVIRONMENT 239375308 (lights on empty football stadium, 5791x3866), all 'Adobe Stock Standard', state just_purchased.

items.question: Does each listed plate record its full-resolution dimensions and license type (Adobe Stock Standard), establishing the assets were genuinely licensed, not scraped?
items.guidance: Yes = every plate row has a full-res WxH and 'Adobe Stock Standard' license. No = missing resolutions or license type, or vague 'downloaded' wording.
items.reference: Each plate row lists full-res dimensions (e.g. 3604x5406) and 'Adobe Stock Standard' license; the note states all four returned license state 'just_purchased' via asset_license_and_download_stock.

items.question: Does the README honestly disclose the build's real limitations (photographic plate scaled to fit not native 300dpi, PDF trim box set at prepress, cast rendered as silhouettes, contact shadows composited locally)?
items.guidance: 5 = the README clearly enumerates each limitation without overclaiming native 300dpi print-res or final CMYK. 1 = limitations hidden or the deliverable is overclaimed as fully native 24x36/300dpi CMYK.
items.reference: README 'Honest limitations' transparently states: photographic plate is ~2.6k px scaled into a 4800x7200/200dpi master (not native 300dpi), 24x36 trim box + SWOP CMYK set at the print house, cast intentionally rendered as silhouettes, contact shadows + multi-element layout composited locally.

task_level.question: Across the deliverables, has EVERY AI region of the approved comp (hero, foil, dusk sky, stadium environment) been rebuilt from real licensed Adobe Stock rather than left as the original AI render?
task_level.guidance: 5 = all four regions are demonstrably sourced from the four licensed Stock plates (documented in the usage note) and visible in the final poster as photographic sky + stadium with cut/graded figures. 1 = any region is still the original AI pixels or undocumented.
task_level.reference: Four plates licensed and consumed (hero 115155591, foil 473425796, sky 654020866, env 239375308); the final poster shows the real twilight sky and lit stadium with the figures cut and re-rendered as graded silhouettes — every AI region rebuilt with Stock per the brief.

task_level.question: Do all composited elements share ONE cohesive warm-key cinematic dusk grade (matching the mood reference) rather than reading as separately-lit cut-outs?
task_level.guidance: 5 = sky, stadium and figures all carry the same warm-highlight/cool-shadow amber-dusk look, consistent with the mood_lighting_ref. 1 = mismatched colour temperatures, one element obviously a different grade, or no unifying look.
task_level.reference: All four plates carry one shared Lightroom preset ('Creative - Cool Shadows & Warm Highlights') plus a matched 9-step relight; the final composite reads as a single warmly-lit dusk set echoing the mood reference's warm key + cool rim.

task_level.question: Is every piece of on-poster copy faithful to the locked source (title, tagline, premiere line, network bug, full billing block) and read programmatically rather than retyped by hand?
task_level.guidance: Yes = all strings match work/copy.json verbatim, including the full 6-line billing block and the V-restored 'VANTAGE NETWORK', with no typos introduced. No = any string retyped wrong, missing, or the input's 'ANTAGE' defect left uncorrected in the final poster.
task_level.reference: work/copy.json holds the verbatim locked copy (title, tagline 'ONE ARENA. ONE WINNER.', premiere 'SERIES PREMIERE THURSDAY 9/8c', bug 'VANTAGE NETWORK', 6-line billing block); the final poster reproduces these correctly and restores the dropped 'V'. (Caveat: the separate logo proof still shows '.AST'.)

task_level.question: Were both required final deliverables produced — a genuine print-ready InDesign PDF (24x36 proportion / 300dpi target) AND a flattened web JPG — plus the stock-usage note and re-vectorized lockup?
task_level.guidance: Yes = the InDesign PDF, the 1200x1800 web JPG, the STOCK_USAGE_NOTE and the vectorized lockup (SVG + proof) are all present. No = any required deliverable missing.
task_level.reference: Present: LAST_ONE_STANDING_24x36_InDesign_print.pdf (genuine InDesign), LAST_ONE_STANDING_web_1200x1800.jpg, STOCK_USAGE_NOTE.md, and the logo lockup SVG + proof PNG — all required deliverables shipped.

task_level.question: Does the evidence show the prescribed Adobe connector workflow was genuinely executed (Stock search/license, per-plate auto-tone/straighten/crop, subject-cut, sky select + depth blur, multi-step relight + shared preset, vectorize, generative-expand, convert/render PDF)?
task_level.guidance: 5 = README + manifest document ~25 distinct connector tools across the full chain matching the prescribed mcp_workflow, with intermediates in work/. 1 = the chain was faked, skipped, or only local PIL was used.
task_level.reference: README lists 25 distinct connector tools / ~37 calls (asset_search x5, asset_license_and_download_stock x4, auto_tone, auto_straighten, crop_to_bounds, select_subject x2, remove_background x2, select_by_prompt x3, gaussian/lens blur, 9 adjust ops, apply_preset x4, color_overlay, fill_area, font_recommend, image_vectorize, generative_expand, crop_and_resize, document_convert_pdf, document_render_layout) matching the prescribed mcp_workflow.

task_level.question: Do the final files honor the print spec (24x36 2:3 proportion, web JPG 1200x1800, bleed + crop marks present) with any shortfalls in native 300dpi/CMYK honestly disclosed rather than overclaimed?
task_level.guidance: 5 = master is true 2:3, web JPG is exactly 1200x1800, a bleed/crop-mark prepress file exists, and the README openly flags that native 300dpi and SWOP CMYK are completed at the print house. 1 = wrong sizes, no bleed handling, or a false claim of finished native 300dpi CMYK output.
task_level.reference: Master 4800x7200 (2:3), bleed file 4850x7250 with crop marks, web JPG exactly 1200x1800 per print_spec; README discloses the photographic plate is ~2.6k px scaled into a 200dpi master and that 24x36 trim box + SWOP CMYK are set at prepress — specs honored with honest disclosure.

tasks.title: Tech-house single release campaign — FX streaming cover (duotone + Ben-Day halftone + glitch alt + film grain), colour-splash club promo photos, vectorized monochrome logo lockup, and Illustrator-merged tour-date merch/sticker cards
tasks.one_line_task: Build the full release-art package for the tech-house single "NIGHTSHIFT": from the raw artist press/live photos and a supplied club-cover reference, deliver a 1600x1600 streaming cover with a duotone + Ben-Day halftone + brand-wash + film-grain treatment plus a VHS-glitch alt colourway, a set of artist promo photos depth-graded to a moody club look with a single magenta colour-splash accent, a vectorized monochrome logo lockup, and a print-ready batch of tour-date merch/sticker cards data-merged from a tour-dates CSV through a client-authored Illustrator Variables template.
tasks.one_line_outputs: 1600x1600 streaming single cover (Apple Music/Spotify spec): hero promo frame rendered as a magenta/black duotone with a Ben-Day halftone screen, a brand-colour wash on multiply, and a film-grain finish; VHS-glitch alt colourway of the cover (chromatic-aberration glitch + added noise) for the visualiser/announce loop; A set of artist promo photos depth-graded to a consistent moody club look (subject popped off a soft-bokeh background, crushed blacks, neon cyan/magenta push)
deliverables: 1600x1600 streaming single cover (Apple Music/Spotify spec): hero promo frame rendered as a magenta/black duotone with a Ben-Day halftone screen, a brand-colour wash on multiply, and a film-grain finish
VHS-glitch alt colourway of the cover (chromatic-aberration glitch + added noise) for the visualiser/announce loop
A set of artist promo photos depth-graded to a consistent moody club look (subject popped off a soft-bokeh background, crushed blacks, neon cyan/magenta push)
A selective-colour / colour-splash treatment isolating one brand accent (magenta neon) on the promo photos, with everything else duotoned
A vectorized monochrome SVG logo lockup re-rendered to print-clean PNG/PDF for cover + merch use
A print-ready batch of tour-date merch / sticker cards (one per tour date) data-merged from the dates CSV via a client-authored Illustrator Variables .ai, exported as PDF + PNG
1600x1600 square + 1080x1920 story social crops of the finished cover for the release announce
tasks.brief: Release-art bundle for the tech-house single "NIGHTSHIFT". From 10 raw club press/live frames and a supplied cover reference, deliver a 1600x1600 streaming cover (magenta/black duotone + Ben-Day halftone + brand-wash + film grain), a VHS-glitch alt colourway, a set of promo photos depth-graded to a moody club look with a single magenta colour-splash accent, a vectorized monochrome logo lockup re-rendered print-clean, and a print-ready batch of tour-date merch/sticker cards data-merged from a 12-row tour-dates CSV through a client-authored Illustrator Variables template, plus 1:1 + 9:16 social crops.
tasks.mcp_workflow: asset_initialize_file_upload -> asset_finalize_file_upload -> asset_search -> asset_license_and_download_stock -> image_select_subject -> image_apply_lens_blur -> image_adjust_exposure -> image_adjust_dark_portions -> image_adjust_hsl -> image_select_by_prompt -> image_adjust_single_color_saturation -> image_invert_selection -> image_apply_monochromatic_tint -> image_apply_monochromatic_tint -> image_apply_halftone -> image_apply_color_overlay -> image_add_grain -> image_apply_glitch_effect -> image_add_noise -> font_recommend -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector -> document_merge_data_vector -> document_render_vector -> image_select_subject -> image_invert_selection -> image_fill_area -> image_crop_and_resize
