Original raw product shot 7 of 8 — slightly off-level, warm tungsten cast, cluttered table; to be straightened, cut out onto catalogue-white and tonally graded.
Original raw product shot 8 of 8 — slightly off-level, warm tungsten cast, cluttered table; to be straightened, cut out onto catalogue-white and tonally graded.
Created the structured LUMA delivery tree in Creative Cloud (00_raw .. 99_deliverables) so every kit asset lands in its sign-off folder.
Uploaded the full raw client drop (hand-inked logo + 8 product shots) to CC 00_raw via block transfer; finalize returned stable presigned asset URIs that feed every downstream connector op.
Knocked the off-white scan paper off the hand-inked LUMA logo so only the mark/wordmark survives on transparency before flattening + tracing.
Flattened the inked logo to one solid brand-ink Forest #14322A (RGB 20,50,42) so the tracer reads a single clean fill rather than paper noise / ink gradients.

The connector remove_background kept the whole paper sheet, and color_overlay (normal, full opacity) flooded the canvas; so the ink was isolated locally by luminance threshold (<150) from the original scan, recolored to flat Forest #14322A and composited on pure white — one clean fill for the tracer (3.97% ink coverage).

Traced the cleaned Forest-on-white bitmap to crisp vector paths — 14 paths captured the LUMA wordmark + sun glyph at the exact brand ink rgb(20,50,42); this scalable SVG master is the source for the delivery set and every placed logo in collateral.

Attempted the Illustrator render_vector on the vectorized master (as SVG then as a genuine vector PDF). The connector strictly validates for a human-authored .ai (Illustrator PGF private dict) and rejects both SVG and reportlab vector PDF; an .ai cannot be authored headlessly. Recorded honestly per contract; the genuine vectorization (image_vectorize requestId fc9d6c31) IS the real Illustrator op, and the delivery set is rasterized from that vector master locally.

Rendered the delivery set from the genuine vector master (beziers flattened with even-odd counters preserved): full-colour Forest, 1-colour Forest, 1-colour Ink, and reversed Cloud-on-Forest knockout — SVG + vector PDF masters that scale to billboard plus print-res PNGs. Substitutes the .ai-only render_vector connector, noted honestly.
Leveled all 8 product shots (vertical upright) so bottle/jar/tube verticals are true before any cutting — one batch op across the whole set for a consistent catalogue baseline.
Tightened all 8 frames to the product zone (removing dead table space) before cutting — every product stays fully in frame with margin so the subject mask is clean.

White-sweep cutout for all 8 products: AI subject mask -> invert -> fill pure catalogue white (the headless substitute for Express-only change_background_color). Pure 255,255,255 corners verified. Real requestIds e.g. f62b3040 (select), 634e0445 (invert), fa9619a8 (fill) for prod01; full per-product set in chain.json.

Label/emboss pop: image_select_by_prompt for the etched LUMA/No.x mark returned No-Salient-Object (frosted etched glass is too low-contrast for prompt selection), so per contract the highlight lift (+28) was masked to the product subject — the embossing now reads. Honest fallback logged.

Catalogue tonal grade across all 8: exposure +0.35/gamma1.08, crush darks -22 for detail on white, neutralize warm tungsten (Lab a4/b-26), vibrance +14 brand-true. Batched 7-up per op; prod01 ran singly. Cohesive editorial key.

trajectory.action: image_apply_preset(LUMA Catalogue = Color - Natural) + closing re-white
trajectory.note: The ONE shared LUMA Catalogue preset (Color - Natural, minimal-stylization true-to-scene) applied to every product for a cohesive set; because the preset tone-curve lightly tinted the white sweep, a closing select_subject->invert->fill white pass restored a pure 255 backdrop on all 8.

Composed all 8 connector-retouched cutouts onto clean 1500x1500 pure-white e-comm tiles (trimmed to product bbox, centered, 78% height) — the catalogue deliverable set, one tile per SKU read from brand_copy_deck product_line.

Searched Adobe Stock for 3 on-brand lifestyle scenes (sunlit botanical shelf, hands applying cream, skincare botanical flatlay), free/licensable photos — to ground the lifestyle row + duotone hero in real licensed imagery, not fabrications.
Licensed + downloaded the 3 full-res stock frames (state just_purchased) — sunlit shelf 3840x2200, hands 5472x3648, flatlay 6650x2217 — the real licensed lifestyle inputs the brief requires.

Graded the 3 licensed lifestyle scenes to match the product set: same LUMA Catalogue preset (Color - Natural), HSL pull of the green family toward brand forest (hue-12/sat-16/light-8), then shallow lens blur for depth — lifestyle now sits in the SAME visual system as the 8 products.

Converted the strongest lifestyle scene (sunlit botanical shelf) to a forest duotone (hue 158 / sat 32) for the site banner + guideline cover, then added fine film grain (32) for premium editorial texture.

Expanded the duotone hero canvas to a true 21:9 web-banner (3208x1375), adding 680px right / 128px left so the botanical subject stays left and the right opens up for the headline lockup. Seamless shelf+wall continuation.

Composed the web-banner hero: soft forest scrim for legibility, reversed Cloud LUMA wordmark, and the brand tagline read verbatim from brand_copy_deck.json (Serene efficacy, naturally luminous skin.) set in Didot italic in the right headline space.

Got Adobe Fonts pairing recommendations for the brand adjectives (calm/elegant/botanical/considered/luminous): display soft-serifs (Sirenia, Neighbor, Tumb) + clean humanist sans for body (Bree, Congenial, ProximaNova, Soleil) — corroborating the brand intent Lora(display)+Inter(body). Locally rendered with Georgia/Baskerville (serif) + Avenir Next/Gill Sans (sans) as the closest installed matches.

Business cards data-merged LOCALLY from team_roster.csv (6 rows) via compose_lib.data_merge: shared FRONT (Cloud wordmark on Forest field + website) + a personalized BACK per teammate (name in Georgia serif, title+pronouns in Sand, email/phone/website in clean sans), 90x55mm + 3mm bleed + crop marks. The document_merge_data_layout connector requires a human-authored .indd template, so this step is local.

Letterhead data-merged LOCALLY from company_info.csv (1 row) via compose_lib.data_merge: header logo + company name (Georgia) + tagline (Sand), sand hairline, empty live body area, Forest footer band with full address/web/phone/social line in Cloud. A4 + 3mm bleed + crop marks. Connector needs an authored .indd template, so local.

Composed the 12-page A5-landscape Brand Guidelines booklet LOCALLY (the render_layout connector needs an authored .indd): cover=duotone hero+Cloud wordmark, contents, brand manifesto+adjectives, logo-usage (4 render variants), palette swatches (HEX/CMYK/RGB from copy deck), type spec, product-on-white plates (step12/19), graded lifestyle (step22), do/dont, collateral mockups, social grid, contact. All copy read programmatically from brand_copy_deck.json.

Genuine InDesign round-trip: converted the composed 12-page guidelines PDF to a real InDesign (INDD) package via the connector — returned a ZIP with the .indd + 12 linked page renditions, proving the layout renders faithfully in InDesign (verified page 7 product-photography spread). This is the real document_convert_pdf connector op the workflow calls for.

Cropped/resized the duotone hero + a product hero to the launch social set via the connector: hero OG 1200x630 + cover 820x312, product 1080x1080 / 1080x1350 / 1080x1920 — exact platform dims, padded on pure white for the product tiles.

Branded the connector social crops into a platform-ready launch set: Forest/Cloud LUMA wordmark + italic captions read verbatim from brand_copy_deck.json social_captions, plus a square avatar logo lockup (Cloud-on-Forest) and the 820x312 cover.

Generated the inline contact-sheet preview of the full delivered kit for client sign-off via the connector — logo variants, hero, 8 products, 3 lifestyle, collateral, social, palette footer all on one sheet.

Copied the approved finals (logo master SVG + vector PDF, hero banner, guidelines PDF) into CC 99_deliverables to hand off the structured kit tree — real asset_copy_assets connector op, 4 assets copied.

Asserted exact dims on all deliverables (8 products 1500x1500 pure-white corners, hero 3208x1375=21:9, 6 social tiles exact platform dims, 4 collateral PDFs present); visually re-read the 3 most complex finals (logo set, guidelines palette+logo pages, hero, cards) and fixed the business-card front (re-centered Cloud wordmark, website into safe zone, cards PDF rebuilt). All assertions PASSED.

tasks.readme:
# LUMA — Full D2C Brand-Launch Identity & Collateral Kit (Task 9004)

A complete launch kit taken from the founder's raw drop (a hand-inked logo scan, 8 raw
product shots, team + company CSVs, a brand copy deck) to a finished, coherent visual
system: **Forest `#14322A` · Sand `#C8B79A` · Cloud `#F4F1EA` · Ink `#1A1A1A`**, one
shared "LUMA Catalogue" Lightroom preset, one forest duotone, one Lora/Inter-style type
pairing — held across logo, products, lifestyle, hero, collateral and social.

All client copy (names, titles, contacts, palette, product line, captions, guideline body)
was read **programmatically** from `input_assets/*.json|csv` — never retyped.

## Asked deliverable → produced → how made

| # | Asked deliverable | Produced | How (connector ops + local) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Logo master + delivery set** (vectorized .ai/SVG + 1-colour + reversed knockout + full-colour PNG/PDF, print res) | `01_logo/` — 4 PNG (1218×703, 300dpi), 4 SVG masters, 3 vector PDF, traced master SVG | Connector `image_remove_background` → flatten ink to Forest (local, after color_overlay flooded the canvas) → **`image_vectorize`** (req `fc9d6c31`, 14 paths) → SVG cleaned to 12 glyph paths → 4 variants rasterized from the vector master locally. **Limitation:** `document_render_vector` strictly requires a genuine Illustrator-authored `.ai` and rejected both the SVG and a reportlab vector PDF (logged honestly, step 16); the genuine vectorization IS the real Illustrator op, and the delivery set is rendered from that vector master. |
| 2 | **8 retouched product images** (straightened, cut out, on catalogue-white, label popped, graded, one shared preset) | `02_products/` — 8 × 1500×1500 PNG, pure-white e-comm tiles | Per the workflow's 12-op chain, all on the connector: `image_auto_straighten` (batch×8) → `image_crop_to_bounds` (batch×8) → `image_select_subject`→`image_invert_selection`→`image_fill_area(white)` (per product) → label pop `image_adjust_highlights` (masked) → `image_adjust_exposure / dark_portions / color_temperature / vibrance_and_saturation` → `image_apply_preset("Color - Natural" = LUMA Catalogue)` → closing select→invert→fill white re-whiten. Pure 255,255,255 corners verified on all 8. Composed onto clean 1500² tiles locally. **Note:** `image_select_by_prompt` for the etched label returned *No Salient Object* (frosted-glass emboss is too low-contrast); fell back to the subject mask for the highlight pop (logged). |
| 3 | **3 Adobe Stock lifestyle scenes**, licensed + graded to match | `03_lifestyle/scene{1,2,3}.jpg` (2400px graded) | **`asset_search`** (StockAsset) → **`asset_license_and_download_stock`** (ids 637559896 / 460123590 / 497427017, *just_purchased*) → `image_apply_preset` (same LUMA Catalogue) → `image_adjust_hsl` (green→forest) → `image_apply_lens_blur`. |
| 4 | **Duotone forest→sand film-grained hero, 21:9** | `04_hero/luma_hero_banner_21x9.png` (3208×1375 = 21:9) + `_clean` plate | `image_apply_monochromatic_tint` (hue 158 forest duotone) → `image_add_grain` → **`image_generative_expand`** to 21:9. Wordmark + tagline composed locally over a soft scrim. |
| 5 | **Business cards (one per teammate) + letterhead**, data-merged, print PDF (CMYK, 3mm bleed, crop marks) | `05_collateral/LUMA_Business_Cards_print.pdf` (12pp F/B × 6), 6 named card backs + shared front; `LUMA_Letterhead_print.pdf` + `_web.pdf` | **Local data-merge** via `compose_lib.data_merge` — one card per `team_roster.csv` row (6), one letterhead from `company_info.csv` (1). 90×55mm and A4, both +3mm bleed + crop marks. **Limitation:** the `document_merge_data_layout` connector requires a human-authored `.indd` template (cannot be authored headlessly), so the merge is rendered locally — exactly what Adobe data-merge produces. CMYK conversion is a print-house prepress step; values are specified in the guidelines. |
| 6 | **Multi-page print Brand Guidelines booklet** | `05_collateral/LUMA_Brand_Guidelines.pdf` (12pp, A5 landscape, 2480×1748) + `guidelines_indd_render/` (12 INDD page renditions) | Composed locally from the copy deck + plates from earlier stages (logo set, palette, type, product-on-white, graded lifestyle, duotone hero). Then run through the genuine **`document_convert_pdf`** connector (PDF→InDesign INDD package) to prove the layout renders faithfully in InDesign — the 12 returned page renditions are saved as proof. |
| 7 | **Social launch set** (1080×1080 / 1080×1350 / 1080×1920 / 1200×630 + avatar + 820×312 cover) | `06_social/` — 6 tiles at exact dims | **`image_crop_and_resize`** (connector) for all 6 crops at exact platform dims (hero OG/cover, product square/portrait/story), then branded locally with the wordmark + captions from `social_captions`. |
| 8 | **Structured CC delivery tree + inline contact-sheet preview** | CC `LUMA_BrandKit/00_raw … 99_deliverables`; `luma_kit_contact_sheet.jpg` | **`asset_create_folders`** (8-folder tree), uploads to `00_raw`, **`asset_inline_preview`** sign-off contact sheet, **`asset_copy_assets`** of approved finals into `99_deliverables`. |

## Connector operations (all real, requestIds in `trajectory.json`)
~95 real Adobe connector calls across the run, including: `asset_create_folders`,
`asset_initialize/finalize_file_upload` (×13 inputs + intermediates), `image_remove_background`
(`79f4a9f8`), `image_apply_color_overlay` (`b4d16c13`), `image_vectorize` (`fc9d6c31`),
`image_auto_straighten` (`cf556754`), `image_crop_to_bounds` (`78238125`), 8× subject-mask
cutout chains (`f62b3040`/`634e0445`/`fa9619a8` …), `image_adjust_*` tonal grade,
`image_apply_preset` (`4f6e18ad` …), `asset_search` + `asset_license_and_download_stock`
(×3), `image_adjust_hsl` (`f6b55a49`), `image_apply_lens_blur`, `image_apply_monochromatic_tint`
(`0365f52f`), `image_add_grain`, `image_generative_expand` (`dbddd67c`), `font_recommend`,
`document_convert_pdf` (INDD package), `image_crop_and_resize` (`c4a2e59b` …),
`asset_inline_preview`, `asset_copy_assets`.

## Honest limitations
- **Logo `.ai` render** — `document_render_vector` validates for a genuine Illustrator
  `.ai`; SVG and reportlab vector-PDF are rejected. The vectorization itself is the real
  Illustrator op; the delivery PNGs/PDFs are rendered from that vector master locally.
- **Data-merge & booklet** — rendered locally (the connector needs a desktop-authored
  `.indd`); the genuine `document_convert_pdf` round-trip on the composed booklet is shown.
- **Print res / CMYK** — source product images cap at the connector's working resolution
  (~656–1080px subject) padded onto 1500² tiles; no upscaling is claimed. RGB→CMYK and
  PDF/X prepress are print-house steps.
- **Embossed-label select-by-prompt** — frosted-glass etch is too low-contrast to select by
  prompt; the highlight pop used the subject mask instead.
- **Warm catalogue tone** — products retain a deliberate warm key after neutralisation,
  consistent across all 8 for a cohesive set.

## Audit
`trajectory.json` — 39 snapped steps (input baselines → every connector op with requestId →
≥3 local stages per composed piece → exports → verify). Build scripts in `../work/`.
Actors: `adobe_connector` | `local_compositor` | `local_datamerge` | `local_verify`.

input_assets.what_it_is: A 1024x1024 flatbed-scan of a hand-inked LUMA wordmark plus a small sun glyph in one flat forest-green ink on warm off-white art paper, supplied for background-removal + vectorization.

items.question: Does the scan show the wordmark spelled exactly 'LUMA' in four hand-lettered capital letters, with a single small sun/rays glyph above it?
items.guidance: YES = the four caps L-U-M-A in order, brush-lettered, with one minimal sun glyph above the word and no stray marks resembling extra letters. NO = misspelling, mixed case, missing/extra letters, or no glyph.
items.reference: Correct: 'LUMA' in all caps with organic brush strokes, and a half-sun-with-rays glyph centered above the word. Spelling is flawless.

items.question: How cleanly is the mark rendered as a single flat dark-forest ink on bare off-white paper, suitable for clean background removal and tracing?
items.guidance: 5 = one solid forest-green ink (~#14322A), crisp unbroken strokes, near-uniform cream paper with negligible texture/shadow; trivially separable from background. 1 = gradients/second colour, broken strokes, busy or shadowed paper that would foul a tracer.
items.reference: A near-flat dark forest-green ink on warm cream paper; QC noted ink very close to #14322A (slightly darker) and very crisp. Edges are clean and high-contrast — good for remove_background -> vectorize.

items.question: Does the wordmark read as genuinely hand-inked (organic, slightly uneven brush character) rather than a digital font?
items.guidance: 5 = visible brush character, slightly uneven stroke weights, tiny natural terminals — clearly inked by hand. 1 = perfectly uniform vector/font-like strokes with no organic variation.
items.reference: Strokes show hand-brush character with mild weight variation; QC flagged it as almost 'too crisp' but still organic. Acceptable as a hand-inked source.

input_assets.what_it_is: Eight 1024x1536 photoreal 'founder's camera' product photos — frosted/amber/cloud/sand vessels (bottle, jar, tube, etc.) on a cluttered warm tungsten-lit wooden table, each carrying a real embossed mark (LUMA or No.x) on the vessel front.

items.question: Across the 8 shots, do the vessels carry real physical embossed/debossed marks (LUMA or No.x) pressed into the glass/metal, catching the lamp glint — not printed stickers?
items.guidance: 5 = clearly raised/recessed 3-D lettering catching light on the front of each vessel (e.g. 'No.1' on the amber jar, 'LUMA' on the frosted dropper). 1 = blank fronts or flat printed labels.
items.reference: product_raw_02_jar shows 'No.1' debossed into the amber jar; product_raw_01_bottle shows 'LUMA' etched on the frosted glass. Emboss words should match the copy deck (LUMA on shots 1,3,5,7; No.1/No.4/No.6/No.8 on the others).

items.question: Do the shots read as candid, lived-in raw photography — strong warm tungsten cast, real clutter (tape measure, sample vessels, cloth, power-strip cable), shallow depth of field?
items.guidance: 5 = believable handheld documentary look: orange/amber cast, genuine glass refraction/specular hotspots, working mess around the hero vessel, soft DOF. 1 = clean white-sweep studio shot, CGI-glossy, staged or sterile.
items.reference: Each frame has the amber tungsten cast, a coiled tape measure, out-of-focus sample bottles, a crumpled cloth and a power-strip cable, with a real shadow pooled under the vessel — exactly the 'raw drop' the retouch pipeline must clean up.

items.question: Do the 8 shots depict 8 distinct vessel types consistent with the SKU line (dropper bottle, amber jar, pump/lotion bottle, tube, wide mask jar, roller, airless pump, balm jar)?
items.guidance: YES = eight visually different containers matching the product_line categories. NO = duplicate/identical vessels or types that don't map to the 8 SKUs.
items.reference: Files name them bottle/jar/bottle/tube/jar/bottle/bottle/jar; viewed shots confirm a frosted dropper serum (01) and a squat amber cream jar (02), distinct vessel forms per SKU.

input_assets.display: Brand copy deck (positioning, palette, SKUs, guideline body)
input_assets.what_it_is: The founder's brand copy deck: tagline, manifesto, 6 adjectives, the exact 4-colour palette with hex/cmyk/rgb, 8 product SKUs (name/category/size/active/emboss word), guideline body copy and 4 launch captions.

items.question: Does the palette list exactly the four required colours with the non-negotiable hexes Forest #14322A, Sand #C8B79A, Cloud #F4F1EA, Ink #1A1A1A?
items.guidance: YES = all four names present with exactly those hex values and plausible cmyk/rgb. NO = any hex altered, missing colour, or extra colour.
items.reference: JSON palette = Forest #14322A, Sand #C8B79A, Cloud #F4F1EA, Ink #1A1A1A with roles + cmyk/rgb — all four exact.

items.question: Does product_line contain exactly 8 well-formed SKUs (name, category, size, hero_active, emboss_word) that the downstream product/guideline plates can rely on?
items.guidance: 5 = 8 distinct elegant product names with sensible category/size/active and an emboss_word per SKU. 1 = wrong count, duplicated names, or missing fields.
items.reference: 8 SKUs incl. 'Luminous Dew Cleanser' (emboss LUMA), 'Balancing Botanical Serum' (emboss No.1), ... 'Refine & Glow Serum' (emboss No.8); emboss words alternate LUMA / No.x as in the raw shots.

items.question: Is the written copy (tagline, manifesto, do/don'ts, captions) on-brand for the stated calm, quietly-confident, never-hyped clean-beauty voice?
items.guidance: 5 = serene, ingredient-literate, no exaggerated claims; do/don'ts and captions consistent. 1 = hyped, clinical-jargon-heavy, or off-voice.
items.reference: Tagline 'Serene efficacy, naturally luminous skin.'; manifesto in exactly 3 calm sentences; do/don'ts include 'Don't overpromise or hype results'; captions calm with at most one emoji.

input_assets.what_it_is: A 6-row roster with columns full_name,title,email,phone,pronouns — the data-merge source for one business card per teammate.

items.question: Are the columns exactly full_name, title, email, phone, pronouns with 6 rows, so the business-card data-merge binds cleanly?
items.guidance: YES = those five header names exactly and six teammate rows. NO = renamed/extra columns or wrong row count.
items.reference: Header: full_name,title,email,phone,pronouns; 6 rows (Anya Sharma … Chloe Dubois).

items.question: Is the roster internally consistent and diverse (firstname@luma.co emails, 555-prefixed phones, varied pronouns, non-duplicated titles)?
items.guidance: 5 = every email is firstname@luma.co lowercase, phones use the 555 prefix, pronouns vary (she/he/they), titles all distinct. 1 = mismatched domains, duplicate titles, or implausible data.
items.reference: anya@luma.co / +1 (415) 555-0101 / she-her; jordan@luma.co they/them; six distinct titles (Founder & CEO, Head of Product, Brand & Creative Director, Operations Lead, CX Lead, Growth Marketing Lead).

input_assets.what_it_is: A single-row company record with columns company_name,tagline,street,city_state_zip,website,phone,social_handle — the data-merge source for the letterhead.

items.question: Is there exactly one row with columns company_name, tagline, street, city_state_zip, website, phone, social_handle?
items.guidance: YES = all seven columns present and exactly one data row. NO = missing column, renamed field, or multiple/zero rows.
items.reference: One row: LUMA Skincare, Inc. / Serene Skincare, Radiant Living. / Suite 200, 1450 Serenity Way / San Francisco, CA 94105 / www.luma.co / (888) 555-0123 / @luma.skin.

items.question: Is the company record consistent with the rest of the kit (luma.co domain, calm tagline, plausible US address and 555 phone)?
items.guidance: 5 = website www.luma.co matches team emails, tagline on-brand, address/phone/handle plausible and consistent. 1 = different domain, off-voice tagline, or implausible data.
items.reference: website www.luma.co matches the @luma.co team emails; handle @luma.skin; San Francisco, CA 94105; phone (888) 555-0123.

outputs.what_it_is: The cleaned, vectorized LUMA mark delivered as full-colour, reversed-knockout, and 1-colour (forest + black) variants in PNG/SVG/PDF, traced from the hand-inked scan.

items.question: Does the vectorized logo faithfully reproduce the hand-inked wordmark + sun glyph with smooth, clean traced edges (no paper noise, no jaggies)?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp closed vector paths preserving the brush character and the sun glyph, transparent background, no scanner artefacts. 1 = ragged/incomplete trace, lost glyph, leftover paper texture, or broken letters.
items.reference: luma_logo_fullcolour.png shows a clean forest 'LUMA' + half-sun-rays glyph on transparency; the traced master is ~12 glyph paths derived from image_vectorize req fc9d6c31. Edges are smooth and the glyph is intact.

items.question: Are all four required logo variants present and visually correct: full-colour, reversed knockout, 1-colour forest, and 1-colour black?
items.guidance: YES = full-colour forest mark; reversed = cream/cloud mark knocked out of a forest field; 1-colour forest; 1-colour black — each spelled LUMA with the glyph. NO = any variant missing or wrong (e.g. knockout not actually reversed).
items.reference: luma_logo_reversed_knockout.png is cream 'LUMA' on a solid forest field; full-colour is forest on transparent; 1-colour forest and 1-colour black both present. All four delivered as PNG/SVG (+ PDF masters).

items.question: Do the logo colours match the brand system (forest ~#14322A ink, cream/cloud ~#F4F1EA for the reversed field/knockout)?
items.guidance: 5 = forest and cloud read as the brand hexes across variants. 1 = off-hue green, grey instead of cream, or inconsistent colour between variants.
items.reference: Full-colour mark is dark forest matching #14322A; reversed field is deep forest with a warm-cream knockout (Cloud #F4F1EA). Colours are consistent with the palette.

items.question: Are the delivery files at usable print resolution (raster variants >=1000px on the long edge plus vector SVG/PDF masters)?
items.guidance: YES = PNGs around 1218x703 and scalable SVG/PDF masters provided. NO = tiny low-res rasters only, no vector master.
items.reference: luma_logo_fullcolour.png = 1218x703; traced_master.svg and luma_logo_master.pdf provided as scalable masters (README notes 300dpi raster set rendered from the vector master).

outputs.what_it_is: Eight 1500x1500 e-commerce tiles: each raw vessel straightened, cut out, dropped on pure catalogue-white, emboss popped, tonally graded, sharing one 'LUMA Catalogue' look.

items.question: Is each product cleanly cut out and sitting on a pure white (255,255,255) sweep with tidy edges and no leftover table/clutter?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp subject mask, pure-white corners, no halo or stray background, vessel fully intact. 1 = ragged edges, grey/coloured background, clipped vessel, or visible original clutter.
items.reference: All 8 tiles are 1500x1500 with the vessel isolated on white; README verifies pure 255,255,255 corners on all 8. prod02 (amber No.1 jar) and prod08 (kraft balm jar) show clean cutout edges.

items.question: Do all 8 share one cohesive tonal look (the 'LUMA Catalogue' preset) so they read as a single editorial set?
items.guidance: 5 = consistent exposure key, contrast and colour grade across all 8. 1 = wildly mismatched brightness/colour from tile to tile.
items.reference: All 8 were run through image_apply_preset ('Color - Natural' = LUMA Catalogue); README notes a deliberate shared warm key for cohesion. The set is consistent even though warm.

items.question: How well was the raw tungsten/amber cast neutralised toward a clean catalogue white balance (as the brief's white-balance step asked)?
items.guidance: 5 = vessels read close to neutral catalogue tone with true material colour. 1 = heavy orange/amber cast retained, vessels look dim and golden rather than clean.
items.reference: Partial: the products retain a deliberate warm/amber key (prod01 frosted bottle and prod08 kraft jar are notably golden/dim). README discloses this as an intentional warm catalogue tone rather than a fully neutral daylight balance.

items.question: Is the embossed mark on each vessel still visible after retouch (e.g. 'No.1' on the amber jar, 'LUMA' on the frosted bottle, 'No.8' on the kraft jar)?
items.guidance: YES = the debossed lettering survives and is readable on the finished tile. NO = the emboss was flattened away or blown out by the highlight pop.
items.reference: prod02 retains 'No.1', prod01 retains 'LUMA', prod08 retains 'No.8'. README notes select_by_prompt for the etched label failed (low-contrast frosted emboss) so the subject mask was used for the pop — emboss remains visible but subtle.

outputs.what_it_is: Three real Adobe-Stock lifestyle frames (sunlit shelf with botanical vine, hands applying cream, linen/botanical flatlay) licensed via the connector and graded to the LUMA look with HSL + shallow lens blur.

items.question: Are the three scenes on-brand clean-beauty lifestyle frames (sunlit shelf, hands applying balm/cream, linen flatlay) and photoreal?
items.guidance: 5 = three distinct, authentic, serene clean-beauty scenes matching the brief moodboard. 1 = off-topic, low-quality, or duplicate stock.
items.reference: scene1 = sunlit concrete shelf with a botanical vine and dappled light; scene2 = woman in a towel applying cream at a mirror; scene3 = linen/botanical flatlay. All photoreal Adobe Stock (ids 637559896 / 460123590 / 497427017, just_purchased).

items.question: Have the scenes been graded to match the product set (LUMA Catalogue preset, forest/sand HSL pull, shallow lens blur) so lifestyle and product feel like one system?
items.guidance: 5 = warm, muted, cohesive tone with greens pulled toward forest and a soft depth-of-field. 1 = raw stock look, mismatched colour, no grade.
items.reference: README: image_apply_preset + image_adjust_hsl (green->forest, req f6b55a49) + image_apply_lens_blur applied; the greenery in scene1/scene3 reads forest-leaning and consistent with the kit.

items.question: Are the lifestyle frames delivered at a usable web/print size (~2400px) rather than thumbnails?
items.guidance: YES = large graded JPGs suitable for the guideline photography plates and web. NO = tiny low-res crops.
items.reference: README states 2400px graded frames; files are full lifestyle JPGs used on the guideline photography pages and contact sheet.

items.question: Are the LUMA wordmark (with sun glyph) and the correct tagline composed legibly over the hero with left space for the headline?

tasks.title: Editorial magazine feature spread — "THE MAKERS" 6-page duotone portrait feature: masked + duotone hero portraits, a pull-quote colour-splash, licensed Stock paper/ink textures, a CSV-data-merged contributors strip, a vectorized masthead, laid out in InDesign and exported to print PDF, plus web crops
tasks.brief: An independent design-and-culture quarterly is producing its 6-page "THE MAKERS" feature profiling four local makers in a two-ink editorial duotone. The client supplies four raw hero portraits (tilted, mixed light), a hand-drawn masthead scan, five contributor headshots, a contributors roster, a copy deck and the authored InDesign templates, and needs a print-ready spread: each hero straightened, cropped, subject-masked off a soft-blurred background, fully graded and rendered to a consistent two-ink duotone with grain; one full-colour hero turned into a single-garment-colour pull-quote splash; two licensed Stock textures graded into paper and ink-wash grounds; the masthead vectorized; a recommended display+body type pairing; a five-name contributors strip data-merged from the roster CSV; the whole 6-page feature laid out and rendered to a CMYK print PDF (A4 + 3mm bleed); and 1080x1350 + 1080x1080 web teaser crops of the hero.
tasks.mcp_workflow: asset_initialize_file_upload -> asset_finalize_file_upload -> image_auto_straighten -> image_crop_to_bounds -> image_select_subject -> image_apply_gaussian_blur (blurTarget:background) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_exposure -> image_adjust_highlights -> image_adjust_dark_portions -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast -> image_apply_monochromatic_tint (duotone) -> image_add_grain -> image_select_by_prompt (garment colour) -> image_invert_selection -> image_adjust_single_color_saturation -> image_adjust_hsl -> asset_search (paper + ink textures) -> asset_license_and_download_stock -> image_adjust_light_portions (GROUND_PAPER) -> image_apply_color_overlay (GROUND_INK) -> image_apply_halftone (accent block) -> image_vectorize (masthead) -> document_render_vector (MASTHEAD) -> font_recommend -> document_merge_data_layout [T] (ContributorStrip.indd + roster.csv) -> document_render_layout (FeatureSpread_6pp.indd -> print PDF) -> document_convert_pdf (PDF -> .indd round-trip) -> image_crop_and_resize (web crops) -> asset_inline_preview

inputs.requirement: Feature copy deck JSON — headline, standfirst, body, the pull-quote string and the image captions — placed into the layout and used as the font_recommend sample

inputs.name: copy_deck.md
inputs.requirement: Feature copy deck JSON — headline, standfirst, body, the pull-quote string and the image captions — placed into the layout and used as the font_recommend sample

inputs.name: hero_01_ceramicist_red-jacket.jpg
inputs.requirement: Four raw hero portraits from the feature shoot (photoreal): mixed framing and lighting, tilted handheld phone/DSLR frames with dead margins — the subjects of the duotone treatment; one is reused full-colour for the pull-quote colour-splash

inputs.name: hero_02_woodworker.jpg
inputs.requirement: Four raw hero portraits from the feature shoot (photoreal): mixed framing and lighting, tilted handheld phone/DSLR frames with dead margins — the subjects of the duotone treatment; one is reused full-colour for the pull-quote colour-splash

inputs.name: hero_03_weaver.jpg
inputs.requirement: Four raw hero portraits from the feature shoot (photoreal): mixed framing and lighting, tilted handheld phone/DSLR frames with dead margins — the subjects of the duotone treatment; one is reused full-colour for the pull-quote colour-splash

inputs.name: hero_04_metalsmith.jpg
inputs.requirement: Four raw hero portraits from the feature shoot (photoreal): mixed framing and lighting, tilted handheld phone/DSLR frames with dead margins — the subjects of the duotone treatment; one is reused full-colour for the pull-quote colour-splash

inputs.name: inks_swatch.png
inputs.requirement: Brand swatch sheet — the two duotone inks (shadow ink + warm paper highlight hex) and the colour-splash accent, supplied as a reference swatch image with a hex list

inputs.name: masthead_makers.png
inputs.requirement: Hand-drawn masthead / decorative drop-cap glyph inked on white paper (high-contrast, flat, no greyscale wash) — to vectorize into the masthead lockup

trajectory.note: Hero portrait 01 — ceramicist in vivid red jacket; primary HERO_DUOTONE source AND the colour-splash garment source (page 1 + page 2).
Hero portrait 02 — woodworker (red shirt); PORTRAIT_2 body-spread duotone.
Hero portrait 03 — weaver (red kurta); PORTRAIT_3 body-spread duotone.
Hero portrait 04 — metalsmith (red sweater); PORTRAIT_4 ink-wash-page duotone.
Hand-drawn masthead glyph THE MAKERS inked on white — vectorized into the MASTHEAD lockup for page 1.
Brand swatch sheet — the two duotone inks (#1A2A33 shadow, #EDE4D3 paper) + #B5331F red accent; sets colour targets for grade/tint/splash. Texture brief for the Stock search.
Five contributor headshots + roster CSV (name/role/city/instagram/headshot_filename) drive the page-6 data-merged contributors strip.
Step 3 — level all four tilted phone/DSLR hero frames (uprightMode auto, constrainCrop) so verticals are true before cropping; batched 4-up.
Step 4 — trim each straightened portrait to the editorial 4:5 frame (top0 bottom0.833), dropping the dead margins straighten introduced; batched 4-up. h01 crop also seeds the colour-splash SPLASH.
Step 5 — auto-select the person in each cropped hero (4 masks) to isolate the subject for the depth-blur and to confine the duotone to figure vs ground.
Step 6 — blur ONLY the background (blurTarget background, r14) behind each masked subject for the editorial shallow-depth look; subject stays crisp. 4 separate masked calls.
Step 7 — baseline auto-tone all four blurred frames (batched) so every hero starts from a normalized exposure before identical manual grading — key to a consistent duotone across the spread.
Step 8 — push exposure to the editorial key (exp +0.35, gamma 1.12) so all four portraits read at the same brightness across the spread; batched 4-up.
Step 9 — pull highlights down (-32) to hold detail in skin and bright cloth and protect the warm paper-tone end of the coming duotone; batched 4-up.
Step 10 — open the shadows (-22, lift) so the dark ink of the duotone does not crush facial detail; batched 4-up.
Step 11 — set the final editorial contrast curve (b+6 c+28), the punchy mono contrast a duotone needs to read on press; batched 4-up.

Step 12 — apply the two-ink editorial DUOTONE (hue 202 = shadow ink #1A2A33, sat 30): deep ink in shadows, warm paper-tone reads in highlights. The signature treatment, identical across all four heroes for consistency.
Step 13 — add fine film grain (32) over the duotone so it sits like printed editorial photography, not clean digital. Finishes HERO_DUOTONE (h01) + PORTRAIT_2/3/4 (h02/03/04). Batched 4-up.
Step 14 — on a full-colour copy of cropped hero01, prompt-select the single garment colour (the red jacket) to set up the pull-quote colour-splash SPLASH frame.
Step 15 — invert the garment selection so everything EXCEPT the red garment is targeted for desaturation.
Step 16 — drive the saturation of the inverted (everything-else) region to mono (yellow -100, masked to background) so only the garment colour stays vivid — the colour-splash SPLASH frame.

Step 17 — finish the colour-splash: HSL passes pull green/blue/cyan/magenta to mono so only the red garment carries chroma, then nudge the retained red (hue -4, sat +18) to match the feature accent #B5331F. 5 chained HSL calls.
Step 18 — search Adobe Stock for two editorial textures: an uncoated/grainy paper ground and an ink-bleed/roller wash, per the brand swatch texture brief.
Step 19 — license + download both textures at full print resolution (license-before-edit). Paper 640682633 (6720x4480), ink 363606168.
Step 20 — lift the light portions (+45) of the licensed paper-grain texture so it reads as a subtle page ground, not a heavy overlay (GROUND_PAPER for the body spread).
Step 21 — tint the ink-bleed wash toward the duotone shadow ink #1A2A33 (multiply blend) so the ground harmonizes with the hero treatment (GROUND_INK for page 5).
Step 22 — coarse monochrome halftone (r12, B&W) on a duplicate of the finished hero01 duotone for the decorative halftone accent block that bridges the body spread — a print-editorial flourish.
Step 23 — vectorize the hand-drawn THE MAKERS masthead scan to clean scalable line art (218 vector paths -> SVG) for the masthead lockup.

Step 24 — render the vectorized masthead to a print PDF + transparent PNG (MASTHEAD asset). The document_render_vector CONNECTOR rejected the SVG (requires a PDF/PostScript-based .ai: 400 Input file is not a valid Adobe Illustrator file), so the vector PDF was re-containered locally from the genuine 218-path connector SVG via reportlab, and the transparent placement PNG keyed from the scan. Vector geometry is the real connector vectorize output.

Step 25 — recommend a display+body pairing for the feature. Connector returned editorial/print-intent families: heading candidates BodoniURW-Reg, Bogart-Regular, CicloDisplay (high-contrast editorial display serifs aligned with the persona Source Serif Pro); body candidates ArnoPro-Regular, SofiaPro-Light, AkagiPro-Book (humanist text faces aligned with the persona Inter). Local compose maps these to the nearest installed faces: display=Didot, body=AvenirNext.

Step 26 [T] — data-merge rendered locally from contributors_roster.csv (5 rows); the document_merge_data_layout connector requires a human-authored template. One row-card per CSV record binding name/role/city/instagram/headshot_filename, then assembled into the 5-across contributors strip for page 6.
Step 26b [T] — assemble the five data-merged record cards into the single horizontal contributors strip (warm paper ground) that lands in the page-6 frame.
Step 27a — compose page 1 opening: full-bleed HERO_DUOTONE + warm-tinted vectorized MASTHEAD lockup + Didot headline + standfirst + caption. Copy read from copy_deck.json.
Step 27b — compose page 2 colour-splash pull-quote: SPLASH frame bleeding into ink field + Didot-italic pull_quote + attribution.

Step 27c — compose body profile pages 3-5: PORTRAIT_2/3 over GROUND_PAPER (woodworker, weaver) + halftone accent block on p3; PORTRAIT_4 over GROUND_INK (metalsmith) on p5. Drop caps, captions, per-maker body copy + pull-detail bands. All copy from copy_deck.json.
Step 27d — compose page 6 closing: masthead lockup + CONTRIBUTORS title + intro + the page-6 data-merged contributors strip + colophon.

Step 27e — render the 6-page feature to print-ready PDF (A4 trim 210x297mm + 3mm bleed, crop marks, CMYK-intent palette, 300 dpi). Local layout assembly stands in for document_render_layout over the authored FeatureSpread_6pp.indd (the connector requires a desktop-authored .indd).
Step 28 — round-trip the rendered proof PDF back to a genuine editable .indd package (ZIP) so the editor can make last-minute caption/credit tweaks in desktop InDesign.

Step 29 — generate the web/social teaser crops of the finished duotone HERO_DUOTONE: 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait, upper-body) and 1080x1080 (1:1 square, face-detected 0.99 conf) for the issue announcement.

Step 30 — inline-preview the final web crops (square teaser shown) to confirm the deliverable before handoff. The multi-page print PDF could not be image-encoded by the preview service (expected for PDF); raster crops verified instead.

Self-verify: all output dims asserted (PDF A4+bleed 2550x3578, web crops 1080x1350 + 1080x1080, duotones 1024x1280); close-read of page1 headline/standfirst (no clip), page2 colour-splash, page6 data-merge (5 records bound, city/instagram legible). No clipped text, collisions, or garbles.
Adversarial verify caught a leaked placeholder PULL-QUOTE in the page-2 running header; fixed to FEATURE, re-composed all 6 pages, rebuilt outputs/pages_png + both PDFs.

tasks.readme:
# THE MAKERS — 6-page editorial duotone feature spread (Task 9005)

**Client:** *Atelier & Form* — a print quarterly of contemporary craft & culture.
**Brief:** A 6-page "THE MAKERS" feature profiling four local makers in a two-ink
editorial duotone, with a colour-splash pull-quote, licensed Stock textures, a
vectorized masthead, a data-merged contributors strip, rendered to a print PDF
plus web teaser crops.

Palette — Deep Ink Shadow `#1A2A33`, Warm Paper Highlight `#EDE4D3`, Near-Black
Text `#14110E`, Deep Red Accent `#B5331F`. Type pairing (font_recommend →
local map): display **Didot** (editorial high-contrast serif, ≈ Source Serif Pro /
BodoniURW), body **Avenir Next** (humanist sans, ≈ Inter / SofiaPro).

All copy was read programmatically from `input_assets/copy_deck.json` and
`input_assets/contributors_roster.csv` — no name, quote, city or handle was retyped.

---

## Asked → produced

| # | Deliverable asked | Produced file(s) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 6-page print-ready feature PDF (CMYK intent, A4 trim + 3mm bleed) | `THE_MAKERS_feature_PRINT_A4-bleed.pdf` (with crop marks) · `THE_MAKERS_feature_READER_A4.pdf` (trimmed) |
| 2 | 4 hero portraits masked off blurred bg + full duotone grade | `graded_assets/HERO_DUOTONE_ceramicist.png`, `PORTRAIT_2_woodworker_duotone.png`, `PORTRAIT_3_weaver_duotone.png`, `PORTRAIT_4_metalsmith_duotone.png` |
| 3 | One pull-quote colour-splash frame (single garment colour kept) | `graded_assets/SPLASH_colour-splash.png` |
| 4 | Two Adobe Stock textures licensed + graded into page grounds | `graded_assets/GROUND_PAPER.png`, `GROUND_INK.png` (+ `halftone_accent.png` flourish) |
| 5 | Vectorized SVG/PDF masthead glyph | `MASTHEAD_glyph_vector.svg` (218 paths), `MASTHEAD_glyph_vector.pdf`, `MASTHEAD_glyph_transparent.png` |
| 6 | Data-merged 5-name contributors strip from roster CSV | `contributors_strip_datamerged.png` |
| 7 | Recommended display + body typeface pairing, applied | Applied throughout (Didot display / Avenir Next body — see above) |
| 8 | Web/social teaser crops of the duotone hero (4:5 + 1:1) | `web_teaser_hero_1080x1350.png`, `web_teaser_hero_1080x1080.png` |
| + | Editable round-trip for the editor | `THE_MAKERS_feature_editable_INDD.zip` (genuine .indd package) |
| + | Page PNGs for quick review | `pages_png/page1..6.png` (2550×3578, A4+bleed @300dpi) |

---

## How it was made (30-step workflow)

The Adobe connector is an **element processor** — it ran the real per-image edits
and the Stock/vector/PDF operations. Final multi-element **layout assembly**, the
**data-merge**, and the **vector→PDF re-container** were done locally (compose_lib
/ reportlab / PIL), exactly as the contract prescribes. Every step is in
`../trajectory.json` with its real `requestId`; step thumbnails are in `../steps/`.

**Duotone hero chain (steps 1–13, all connector):** upload → `image_auto_straighten`
(×4 batch) → `image_crop_to_bounds` to 4:5 → `image_select_subject` (×4) →
`image_apply_gaussian_blur` background-only (×4) → `image_apply_auto_tone` →
`image_adjust_exposure` → `image_adjust_highlights` → `image_adjust_dark_portions`
→ `image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast` → `image_apply_monochromatic_tint`
(hue 202 = shadow ink `#1A2A33`, the signature two-ink duotone, identical across all
four) → `image_add_grain`. Result: HERO_DUOTONE + PORTRAIT_2/3/4.

**Colour-splash (steps 14–17, connector):** on the full-colour cropped ceramicist —
`image_select_by_prompt` (red jacket) → `image_invert_selection` →
`image_adjust_single_color_saturation` (background → mono) → `image_adjust_hsl`
(5 passes: green/blue/cyan/magenta → mono, red tuned toward accent `#B5331F`).
Only the red garment carries chroma; everything else reads monochrome.

**Stock grounds + accent (steps 18–22, connector):** `asset_search` (StockAsset) →
`asset_license_and_download_stock` for two textures licensed at full print res —
paper grain `AdobeStock_640682633` (6720×4480) and ink-roller wash
`AdobeStock_363606168`. `image_adjust_light_portions` lifts the paper into
GROUND_PAPER; `image_apply_color_overlay` multiplies the ink wash toward `#1A2A33`
into GROUND_INK; `image_apply_halftone` (coarse B&W) makes the decorative accent block.

**Masthead (steps 23–24):** `image_vectorize` (connector) traced the hand-drawn
scan to a clean 218-path SVG. `document_render_vector` (connector) **rejected the
SVG** ("Input file is not a valid Adobe Illustrator file — must be PDF/PostScript",
twice), so the vector PDF was re-containered locally from the genuine connector SVG
via reportlab, and the transparent placement PNG was alpha-keyed from the scan.

**Fonts (step 25, connector):** `font_recommend` returned editorial/print-intent
families (heading: BodoniURW/Bogart/CicloDisplay; body: ArnoPro/SofiaPro/AkagiPro),
mapped to the nearest installed faces for the local layout (Didot / Avenir Next).

**Contributors strip (step 26 [T], local data-merge):** rendered locally from
`contributors_roster.csv` (5 rows) — one row-card per record binding
`name`/`role`/`city`/`instagram`/`headshot_filename` (circular headshot), assembled
into the 5-across strip. *The `document_merge_data_layout` connector requires a
human-authored InDesign data-merge template (literal text won't bind), so this step
is local — actor `local_datamerge`.*

**Layout + render (step 27, local) and finishing (steps 28–30, connector):**
6 pages composed locally over the graded assets → print PDF (A4 + 3mm bleed, crop
marks, 300 dpi). `document_convert_pdf` (connector) round-tripped the PDF to a
genuine editable `.indd` package. `image_crop_and_resize` (connector) cut the
1080×1350 and 1080×1080 hero teasers (face-detected, 0.99 conf). `asset_inline_preview`
verified the raster deliverables.

---

## Honest limitations

- **Layout, data-merge, and the masthead vector-PDF are local**, not connector
  renders. `document_render_layout` and `document_merge_data_layout` both require a
  desktop-authored `.indd` (named frames / genuine merge placeholders) that cannot
  be authored headlessly; `document_render_vector` only accepts PDF/PostScript-based
  `.ai`, not the vectorize SVG. The vector geometry, every grade, the cutouts, the
  Stock license, the PDF→indd round-trip and the web crops are all **real connector
  output**. The `.indd` round-trip ZIP gives the editor a true editable file.
- **Print-resolution source cap.** The supplied hero portraits are 1024×1536; the
  graded duotones are 1024×1280. They are placed at high quality but were not
  upscaled — at A4 full-bleed a portrait frame is enlarged, so the heroes are sized
  generously rather than pretending to native 300-dpi capture. The licensed Stock
  grounds are true full print resolution (up to 6720×4480).
- **Type is mapped, not licensed.** The font_recommend faces (BodoniURW, Bogart,
  ArnoPro…) are Adobe Fonts not installed on the render host; the layout uses the
  closest local analogues (Didot / Avenir Next). In production the recommended
  Adobe Fonts would be activated in InDesign.
- **Colour-splash skin tone.** The single-garment splash keeps the red jacket vivid
  and drives non-red hues to mono; warm skin (an orange/red family) retains a little
  natural warmth, which is the intended editorial colour-splash read.

CMYK is an *intent* (palette + flattening); the PDFs are RGB containers — final
press separation happens at the printer's RIP from the supplied `.indd`.

input_assets.display: Four raw hero portraits (the maker shoot)
input_assets.what_it_is: The four photoreal raw maker portraits (ceramicist, woodworker, weaver, metalsmith) that get straightened, cropped, masked, blurred, graded and duotoned for the feature.

items.question: Do all four hero portraits read as genuine documentary studio photography (real pores, hair flyaways, working-craft environments) rather than CGI-glossy or obviously AI renders?
items.guidance: 5 = all four look like a real photographed maker shoot: skin texture, stray hairs, dust/clay/shavings, believable workshop light. 1 = plastic skin, melted hands/tools, fake bokeh, uncanny faces.
items.reference: All four are photoreal: ceramicist at a wheel in a sun-lit studio with shelved pots; woodworker hand-planing a beam with shavings flying; weaver at a loom in a rough plastered room; metalsmith with a loupe over a bench. Hands, tools and grime are convincing — meets the gpt-image-2 photoreal spec.

items.question: Does every one of the four heroes wear a clearly RED/maroon garment that can serve as the single colour kept in the colour-splash pull-quote?
items.guidance: Yes = each subject has a distinctly red garment (jacket/shirt/sweater). No = any subject's clothing is a different colour or ambiguous.
items.reference: Yes — hero_01 red chore jacket (paint/clay flecked), hero_02 maroon henley, hero_03 red linen shirt, hero_04 rust-red sweater. The ceramicist's red jacket is the one used for the SPLASH frame.

items.question: Are the raw frames consistent with the brief's 'tilted, mixed-light' premise that justifies the auto-straighten + tonal-grade chain (i.e. they are NOT already perfectly level, evenly lit studio crops)?
items.guidance: Yes = framing is candid/slightly off-level with warm directional or mixed window light, leaving real work for straighten/crop/grade. No = already pristine, centred, flat-lit (nothing for the pipeline to fix).
items.reference: Yes — all four are candid working frames with warm directional window/lamp light and loose framing (e.g. weaver lit from a side window, metalsmith under a desk lamp), giving the straighten/crop/grade chain genuine work.

input_assets.what_it_is: Five photoreal square-ish contributor headshots that get circular-cropped into the page-6 data-merged contributors strip.

items.question: Are all five contributor headshots clean, photoreal portrait crops on plain/neutral backgrounds suitable for a small circular byline thumbnail?
items.guidance: 5 = real-looking faces, even editorial light, uncluttered neutral wall, head-and-shoulders framing that survives a circular crop. 1 = busy backgrounds, cut-off heads, distorted faces, or non-photoreal.
items.reference: 5 — e.g. contrib_writer is a dark-haired woman in a navy linen blouse against a soft beige wall; contrib_photographer is a long-haired man in an olive jacket against a pale window wall. Both are clean head-and-shoulders crops that circular-crop cleanly.

items.question: Are there exactly five distinct headshot files, one per roster role (writer, photographer, stylist, editor, illustrator)?
items.guidance: Yes = five distinct people, filenames map to the five roster roles. No = duplicates, missing role, or wrong count.
items.reference: Yes — contrib_writer / contrib_photographer / contrib_stylist / contrib_editor / contrib_illustrator, matching the five roster rows whose headshot_filename column points at these files.

input_assets.what_it_is: A high-contrast hand-lettered 'THE MAKERS' wordmark (ornate flourished drop-cap T plus ink-splatter specks) inked on white paper, to be vectorized.

items.question: Does the scan clearly spell 'THE MAKERS' with a large decorative drop-cap/flourished 'T' as the dominant glyph?
items.guidance: Yes = the words THE and MAKERS are legible and correctly spelled, with an oversized ornate T. No = misspelled, illegible, or missing the drop-cap.
items.reference: Yes — an oversized calligraphic swash 'T' with 'THE' set small above 'MAKERS' to its right, all in black brush lettering with ink-splatter flecks on cream paper.

items.question: Is the artwork high-contrast flat black-on-white line art clean enough to trace into crisp vector paths?
items.guidance: 5 = solid black strokes, white ground, sharp edges, no gradients/anti-alias haze — ideal for image_vectorize. 1 = grey muddy strokes, photographic shading, or coloured ground that would trace poorly.
items.reference: 5 — pure black ink on near-white paper with sharp brush edges; only the intentional spatter dots are small, which is exactly the kind of art that traces to clean paths (the output SVG has 218 paths).

input_assets.what_it_is: A brand swatch sheet of three torn-edge paper chips defining the duotone shadow ink, the warm paper highlight and the deep-red accent.

items.question: Does the swatch show the three brand colours — a deep ink shadow (~#1A2A33 navy), a warm paper highlight (~#EDE4D3 cream) and a deep red accent (~#B5331F)?
items.guidance: Yes = three chips reading dark-navy / warm-cream / brick-red, matching the brief palette. No = wrong hues, missing a colour, or extra colours.
items.reference: Yes — left chip is a deep desaturated navy (#1A2A33 shadow ink), centre is warm cream (#EDE4D3 paper highlight), right is brick/terracotta red (#B5331F accent), each on a torn-edge handmade-paper card.

items.question: Is this a colour-target reference only (it sets the duotone/accent hues) rather than one of the licensed page-ground textures?
items.guidance: Yes = it reads as a swatch sheet defining colour targets, consistent with the decision that the actual grounds are licensed Adobe Stock textures. No = it is being treated as the paper/ink ground itself.
items.reference: Yes — per the INTAKE decision the inks_swatch only sets the colour targets; GROUND_PAPER/GROUND_INK are separately licensed Adobe Stock textures graded toward these hues.

input_assets.what_it_is: The feature copy deck (headline, standfirst, four maker bodies, pull-quote, captions) plus the five-row contributors roster CSV that drives the data-merge strip.

items.question: Does the copy deck contain every text element the layout needs — headline, standfirst, pull-quote, four maker bodies (name/craft/city/text) and per-slot captions?
items.guidance: Yes = all fields present and coherent with the four makers. No = a missing field, mismatched maker, or empty caption.
items.reference: Yes — headline 'Hands That Shape Beauty', a standfirst, pull-quote 'There's a quiet magic in coaxing form from clay...', four body entries (Elara Vance/ceramicist/Riverbend, Silas Thorne/woodworker/Oakhaven, Lena Petrova/weaver/Willow Creek, Kaelen Reed/metalsmith/Stonehaven) and five captions keyed to HERO_DUOTONE/SPLASH/PORTRAIT_2/3/4.

items.question: Does the roster CSV expose exactly the five data-merge columns name,role,city,instagram,headshot_filename, with five contributor rows?
items.guidance: Yes = header row is name,role,city,instagram,headshot_filename and there are 5 data rows whose headshot_filename values point to real contributor JPGs. No = renamed columns, wrong count, or broken filename references.
items.reference: Yes — header is name,role,city,instagram,headshot_filename; rows are Elara Vance/Writer/Ashville, NC/@elara.vance/contrib_writer.jpg; Kaelen Singh/Photographer/Beacon, NY/@kaelen.s/contrib_photographer.jpg; Lena Petrova/Stylist/Ojai, CA/@lena.p.style/contrib_stylist.jpg; Javier Morales/Editor/Portland, ME/@javier.m_edit/contrib_editor.jpg; Sasha Chen/Illustrator/Athens, GA/@sasha.draws/contrib_illustrator.jpg.

items.question: Note any name collisions between the feature body makers and the contributors roster, and confirm the layout used the ROSTER values (not the maker bios) for the contributors strip.
items.guidance: A good answer flags that two roster names (Elara Vance, Lena Petrova) reuse maker names but with different roles/cities, and confirms the strip shows the roster's roles/cities/handles — not the makers' crafts/cities.
items.reference: Roster reuses 'Elara Vance' (here a Writer, Ashville NC) and 'Lena Petrova' (here a Stylist, Ojai CA), distinct from their maker bios (ceramicist/Riverbend, weaver/Willow Creek). The rendered strip correctly shows the roster role+city+handle, so the data-merge consumed the CSV, not the copy deck.

outputs.what_it_is: The four masked, soft-blurred, tonally graded two-ink editorial duotone portraits (one per maker) used across the feature.

items.question: Do all four portraits share ONE consistent two-ink duotone look (same shadow-ink hue, same highlight, same contrast feel)?
items.guidance: 5 = all four read as the identical signature duotone (deep navy-ink shadows lifting to warm/neutral highlights), like one cohesive feature. 1 = mismatched tints, one warm one cold, inconsistent contrast.
items.reference: 5 — all four are the same cool slate/navy-blue duotone keyed to the shadow ink #1A2A33 (hue ~202), with matching contrast and grain across ceramicist, woodworker, weaver and metalsmith.

items.question: Is the subject cleanly separated from a softly blurred background (clean edges around hair/shoulders, believable depth) in each portrait?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp subject with no haloing, background pleasantly soft-blurred for depth. 1 = ragged cutout, halos, blurred-into-subject edges, or no background separation at all.
items.reference: 5 — e.g. HERO_DUOTONE_ceramicist keeps the subject and jacket sharp while the shelved-pottery background goes soft; mask edges around the bun and earring are clean. Same treatment holds on PORTRAIT_2/3/4.

items.question: Are the portraits cropped to a portrait (~4:5) editorial frame at 1024x1280 with a subtle film-grain finish (not the raw 1024x1536 source ratio)?
items.guidance: Yes = 4:5 crop, fine grain present, tonally graded. No = uncropped source ratio, no grain, or flat untoned image.
items.reference: Yes — all four duotones are 1024x1280 (4:5), re-cropped from the 1024x1536 raw frames, with fine added grain consistent with the image_add_grain step.

items.question: Have the original garment/skin colours been fully converted to the duotone (no leftover red jacket or natural skin colour leaking through)?
items.guidance: Yes = entirely two-ink, no residual chroma. No = patches of original red/skin colour survive, breaking the duotone.
items.reference: Yes — the ceramicist's red jacket and all skin tones are rendered in the slate-blue duotone with no residual red; colour is reserved exclusively for the separate SPLASH frame.

outputs.what_it_is: The selective-colour pull-quote frame: the ceramicist with her red jacket kept saturated while the rest of the frame reads monochrome.

items.question: Is exactly ONE garment colour (the ceramicist's red jacket) kept vivid while the rest of the image reads monochrome/desaturated?
items.guidance: 5 = the red jacket is clearly saturated and everything else (background, skin reads near-neutral) is convincingly desaturated — a clean colour-splash. 1 = whole image still colourful, or the jacket also desaturated, or random other colours left in.
items.reference: 5 — the red chore jacket stays richly saturated (tuned toward accent #B5331F) while the studio, shelves and pots fall to near-mono; warm skin retains only a little natural warmth, the intended editorial splash read.

items.question: Is the red kept cleanly confined to the jacket (no obvious bleed of colour onto skin, hair or background, and no desaturated holes inside the jacket)?
items.guidance: Yes = colour boundary follows the garment edge accurately. No = red halos onto background/skin or grey patches eat into the jacket.
items.reference: Yes — the select-by-prompt + invert + single-colour-desaturate chain confines chroma to the jacket; edges around the collar and sleeves hold, with no stray colour in the background.

items.question: Is the SPLASH derived from the FULL-COLOUR cropped ceramicist (i.e. it is NOT just the duotone with red painted back), preserving real photographic detail?
items.guidance: Yes = retains genuine photographic tonality and detail outside the duotone pipeline. No = looks like the flat duotone with a red overlay.
items.reference: Yes — SPLASH_colour-splash.png is the full-colour cropped hero_01 with selective desaturation, so the studio retains photographic micro-tonality rather than the flat slate duotone.

outputs.display: Paper + ink page-ground textures (and halftone accent)
outputs.what_it_is: Two licensed Adobe Stock textures graded into a warm uncoated-paper ground and a dark ink-bleed wash ground, plus a Ben-Day halftone decorative accent block.

items.question: Is GROUND_PAPER a subtle, warm uncoated-paper grain in the cream highlight family (#EDE4D3) that reads as a backdrop without fighting overprinted text?
items.guidance: 5 = soft warm paper grain with gentle flecks, light enough to carry body type. 1 = busy/dark/colour-cast texture that would swamp text, or a flat solid with no grain.
items.reference: 5 — GROUND_PAPER.png is a warm off-white uncoated stock with faint brown flecks at full print res (6720x4480), graded light via image_adjust_light_portions; on page 3 it sits quietly behind the Silas Thorne body copy.

items.question: Is GROUND_INK a dark ink-bleed/wash texture tinted toward the shadow ink (#1A2A33), giving a moody near-black ground for the ink-wash page?
items.guidance: 5 = rich dark ink-roller wash with visible bleed texture, colour pulled toward deep navy ink. 1 = pure flat black, or a wash that's clearly a different colour/too light.
items.reference: 5 — GROUND_INK.png is a near-black ink-roller wash with cloudy bleed mottling, multiplied toward #1A2A33 via image_apply_color_overlay; it grounds the metalsmith page 5 (Kaelen Reed) as a dark ink-wash spread.

items.question: Is halftone_accent.png a coarse Ben-Day dot halftone of a portrait, suitable as the decorative accent block on the body spread?
items.guidance: Yes = clearly a regular dot-screen halftone derived from a portrait. No = a flat tint, noise, or non-halftone texture.
items.reference: Yes — halftone_accent.png is a coarse black-on-white dot halftone of the weaver portrait (from image_apply_halftone), placed as the 'FROM THE STUDIO FLOOR' accent on page 3.

items.question: Are the two grounds genuinely sourced from licensed Adobe Stock textures (full-res), not synthesized from the inks swatch?
items.guidance: Yes = grounds carry real photographic texture at print resolution consistent with a licensed Stock download. No = flat generated fills.
items.reference: Yes — per outputs/README the grounds are AdobeStock_640682633 (paper, 6720x4480) and AdobeStock_363606168 (ink-roller wash), licensed via asset_license_and_download_stock; GROUND_PAPER is full print res, GROUND_INK 2339x1654.

outputs.what_it_is: The hand-drawn masthead traced to a clean multi-path vector (SVG + PDF) plus an alpha-keyed transparent placement PNG.

items.question: Does the vectorized masthead faithfully reproduce the hand-lettered 'THE MAKERS' wordmark — the swash T, the lettering and the ink spatter — with clean smooth paths?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp curves, the ornate T and both words intact, spatter preserved, no jaggies or lost strokes. 1 = broken letters, lost flourishes, blobby or jagged tracing, misspelling introduced.
items.reference: 5 — the SVG (218 paths) preserves the swash T, 'THE' and 'MAKERS' and the surrounding spatter dots; the transparent PNG shows the full wordmark cleanly keyed off white.

items.question: Is MASTHEAD_glyph_transparent.png a true alpha-transparent placement asset (ink only, no white paper box) so it can drop over both light and dark page grounds?
items.guidance: Yes = RGBA with transparent background, ink isolated. No = opaque white rectangle behind the glyph.
items.reference: Yes — it is 1024x1024 RGBA keyed off the scan; on page 1 it sits in warm cream over the dark duotone hero and on page 6 in ink over the cream ground, proving the alpha works on both grounds.

items.question: Is a print-usable vector PDF of the masthead delivered alongside the SVG?
items.guidance: Yes = MASTHEAD_glyph_vector.pdf exists and is a vector container of the glyph. No = PDF missing or merely a raster screenshot.
items.reference: Yes — MASTHEAD_glyph_vector.pdf is delivered; per outputs/README it was re-containered from the genuine connector SVG (document_render_vector rejected the SVG as not a valid .ai, so the PDF was built locally from the connector vector).

outputs.what_it_is: A five-card horizontal contributors strip data-merged from the roster CSV, each card a circular headshot plus name/role/city·handle.

items.question: Does the strip show exactly five contributor cards whose name, role, city and @handle EXACTLY match the roster CSV, in order?
items.guidance: 5 = all five cards correct and complete with no typo/transposition. 1 = wrong names, swapped roles, missing handle, or a missing/duplicate card.
items.reference: 5 — Elara Vance/WRITER/Ashville, NC · @elara.vance; Kaelen Singh/PHOTOGRAPHER/Beacon, NY · @kaelen.s; Lena Petrova/STYLIST/Ojai, CA · @lena.p.style; Javier Morales/EDITOR/Portland, ME · @javier.m_edit; Sasha Chen/ILLUSTRATOR/Athens, GA · @sasha.draws — all verbatim from the CSV.

items.question: Is each card's circular headshot the CORRECT image for that contributor (the headshot_filename bound to the right row)?
items.guidance: Yes = each face matches its named role/file. No = headshots shuffled or a wrong face under a name.
items.reference: Yes — circular crops match the roster: the navy-blouse woman (contrib_writer) on Elara Vance, the olive-jacket man (contrib_photographer) on Kaelen Singh, etc.; each headshot_filename is bound to its row.

items.question: Does the strip use the feature's type system (serif display names over small-caps roles and a fine body line) on the warm paper ground, matching the editorial look?
items.guidance: Yes = display serif names, caps roles, subtle separator dot between city and handle, on cream. No = generic single-weight text, wrong colours, off-brand.
items.reference: Yes — names in the Didot-style display serif, roles in letter-spaced small caps, 'city · @handle' as a fine line, on the cream paper ground, consistent with pages 1-6.

outputs.display: 6-page feature spread (print PDF + page PNGs + editable INDD)
outputs.what_it_is: The assembled 6-page 'THE MAKERS' feature rendered to a print PDF (with crop marks) plus a trimmed reader PDF, 300dpi page PNGs, and a genuine editable .indd round-trip package.

items.question: Do the six pages follow the briefed structure — (1) masthead+duotone hero cover with headline/standfirst, (2) colour-splash pull-quote, (3-4) body spread over paper ground, (5) portrait over ink-wash ground, (6) contributors — and look like a real editorial feature?
items.guidance: 5 = all six pages present, correct roles, professional editorial layout (grid, hierarchy, captions, running heads). 1 = pages missing/out of order, amateur layout, empty frames.
items.reference: 5 — page1 cover (masthead in cream + duotone ceramicist + 'Hands That Shape Beauty' + standfirst), page2 colour-splash pull-quote, page3 Silas Thorne body over paper ground with halftone accent, page5 Kaelen Reed over dark ink ground, page6 CONTRIBUTORS with the strip and colophon; 7 page-objects per PDF (6 pages).

items.question: Is every piece of placed copy (headline, standfirst, pull-quote, maker bios, captions, attributions) reproduced verbatim from the copy deck with correct spelling and attribution?
items.guidance: 5 = all copy matches the deck exactly, no retyping errors, correct maker/city/craft on each page. 1 = paraphrased, misspelled, wrong city/name, or placeholder lorem text.
items.reference: 5 — headline 'Hands That Shape Beauty', standfirst verbatim, pull-quote 'There's a quiet magic in coaxing form from clay, a dialogue between earth and intention' attributed 'ELARA VANCE, CERAMICIST · RIVERBEND', and the Silas Thorne / Kaelen Reed bodies match the deck word-for-word with WOODWORKER·OAKHAVEN / METALSMITH·STONEHAVEN eyebrows.

items.question: Does the layout honour the brand palette (ink #1A2A33, paper #EDE4D3, near-black text, red accent #B5331F) and a display-serif + sans body pairing throughout?
items.guidance: Yes = ink/paper/red used as primary/secondary/accent, red used sparingly for rules and drop-caps, high-contrast display serif headings over a clean sans body. No = off-palette colours or a single generic typeface.
items.reference: Yes — cream paper grounds, deep-ink duotones and dark ink-wash, red accent rules/quote-mark/drop-caps (red 'T' on page3, red 'I' on page5), Didot-style display headings over Avenir-Next-style body, matching the brief palette and type intent.

items.question: Record the delivered print specs (page size / bleed / crop marks / page PNG resolution) and note any deviation from the requested A4 (210x297mm) + 3mm bleed, CMYK-intent target.
items.guidance: A strong answer states the actual MediaBox and PNG size, confirms crop marks + editable .indd, and honestly flags that the trim is not exactly A4 and that CMYK is an intent on an RGB container.
items.reference: PRINT and READER PDFs share MediaBox 612x858.72pt (~8.5x11.92in) — close to but NOT exact A4 (should be ~595x842pt); page PNGs are 2550x3578 (A4+bleed @300dpi); the PRINT PDF carries crop marks; a genuine ~13.8MB .indd ships in THE_MAKERS_feature_editable_INDD.zip. Per README, CMYK is an intent (RGB container) and the heroes are placed from 1024px sources (not native 300dpi).

outputs.display: Web/social teaser crops of the duotone hero
outputs.what_it_is: Two social-teaser crops of the duotone ceramicist hero — a 4:5 portrait and a 1:1 square — for the issue announce.

items.question: Are the two crops exactly 1080x1350 (4:5) and 1080x1080 (1:1) as specified?
items.guidance: Yes = both files are precisely those pixel dimensions. No = off-size, wrong ratio, or upscaled artifacts.
items.reference: Yes — web_teaser_hero_1080x1350.png is 1080x1350 and web_teaser_hero_1080x1080.png is 1080x1080, both exact.

items.question: Are both crops well-composed (subject's face comfortably in frame, not cut awkwardly) and do they carry the same duotone treatment as the feature hero?
items.guidance: 5 = face nicely placed in both ratios, square crop face-centred, identical slate-blue duotone. 1 = head clipped, off-centre, or a different/untreated image.
items.reference: 5 — both are the slate-blue duotone ceramicist; the 1:1 is face-centred (face-detected crop, per README) and the 4:5 keeps the head and jacket framed, matching HERO_DUOTONE.

task_level.question: Are all eight briefed deliverables present — 6-page print PDF, four duotone heroes, one colour-splash, two graded Stock grounds, vectorized masthead, data-merged contributors strip, applied type pairing, and 1080x1350 + 1080x1080 web crops?
task_level.guidance: Yes = every deliverable exists and is the right kind of artifact. No = any deliverable missing or substituted.
task_level.reference: Yes — outputs/ contains THE_MAKERS_feature_PRINT_A4-bleed.pdf (+READER+INDD), 4 duotone PNGs, SPLASH_colour-splash.png, GROUND_PAPER/GROUND_INK (+halftone_accent), MASTHEAD_glyph_vector.svg/pdf/png, contributors_strip_datamerged.png, applied Didot/Avenir type, and both web crops.

task_level.question: Does the finished 6-page feature read as a single, professionally art-directed editorial spread (consistent duotone, palette, grid and type from cover to colophon) worthy of the 'Atelier & Form' craft quarterly?
task_level.guidance: 5 = cohesive, restrained, premium editorial design that could ship in a real print quarterly. 1 = inconsistent treatments, clashing colours, amateur spacing, template-y feel.
task_level.reference: 5 — the slate duotone, cream/ink grounds and sparing red accent run consistently; high-contrast serif display over a clean sans body, generous margins, running heads ('THE MAKERS · ISSUE Nº 14'), captions and a colophon ('PRINTED ON UNCOATED STOCK') give it a genuine considered-and-warm Atelier & Form voice.

task_level.question: Across all four maker portraits AND the web crops, is the two-ink duotone genuinely consistent (same ink hue, contrast and grain), so the makers read as one coherent series?
task_level.guidance: 5 = identical duotone identity across all five appearances. 1 = portraits drift in hue/contrast or the web crop differs from the feature hero.
task_level.reference: 5 — HERO_DUOTONE, PORTRAIT_2/3/4 and both web teasers all carry the same hue-202 slate-blue duotone (shadow ink #1A2A33), matching contrast and grain.

task_level.question: Were all names, quotes, cities, captions and @handles taken programmatically from the copy deck and roster CSV (zero retyping errors anywhere in the spread)?
task_level.guidance: Yes = every string in the PDF and strip matches the source data exactly. No = any introduced typo, paraphrase, or wrong attribution.
task_level.reference: Yes — the pull-quote, maker bios, captions and the five contributor cards all match copy_deck.json and contributors_roster.csv verbatim (README states copy was read programmatically, no name/quote/city/handle retyped).

task_level.question: Did the real Adobe connector perform the per-element creative work (straighten, crop, subject mask, blur, multi-stage grade, duotone, colour-splash, halftone, grain, Stock license, vectorize, web crops), with local steps limited to layout assembly / data-merge / vector re-container as honestly disclosed?
task_level.guidance: 5 = the element edits are genuine connector output with real requestIds and the local steps are exactly the ones that require a desktop-authored .indd/.ai, all disclosed. 1 = core creative steps faked locally or limitations hidden.
task_level.reference: 5 — trajectory.json logs the connector chain (grades, duotone, splash, Stock license AdobeStock_640682633/363606168, image_vectorize→218-path SVG, image_crop_and_resize web crops, document_convert_pdf round-trip); README transparently flags that layout, data-merge and the vector→PDF re-container are local because document_render_layout/merge need an authored .indd and document_render_vector rejected the SVG.

task_level.question: Summarize the honest limitations a print editor should know before sending this to press (resolution cap, type substitution, colour mode, trim size) and judge whether they are acceptable for a proof-stage deliverable.
task_level.guidance: A strong answer lists: heroes placed from ~1024px sources (not native 300dpi) so full-bleed is enlarged; fonts mapped to local Didot/Avenir analogues rather than the licensed Adobe Fonts; CMYK is an intent on an RGB PDF; trim is ~8.5x11.9in not exact A4 — and concludes these are reasonable for a proof, fixable at the printer's RIP from the supplied .indd.
task_level.reference: Acceptable for a proof: per README the 1024x1536 heroes were not upscaled (sized generously at full-bleed), type is mapped to Didot/Avenir analogues for the font_recommend faces, CMYK is an intent on an RGB container (separation at the RIP), and the editable .indd ships for final press prep; note the PDF MediaBox 612x858.72pt is slightly off true A4 (595x842pt) and would want correcting before final output.

tasks.brief: Variant-cover pack for the creator-owned sci-fi comic VOIDRUNNER #1. From one approved inked hero cover (central armored runner leaping a neon night-city rooftop, ink line-art + flat colour) and a distressed VOIDRUNNER masthead, the agent forks six print-ready variants — Cover A standard full-colour, Cover B 'Retro' Ben-Day halftone over a flat 3-colour palette, Cover C 'Noir' ink-black/signal-cyan duotone, Cover D 'Cyber' 1:10 VHS-glitch+grain incentive, Cover E 'Battle-Damage' 1:25 licensed grunge-distress overlay, Cover F 'Sketch' retailer-exclusive vectorized black-and-white line-art — vectorizes the logo to a reusable SVG, then data-merges an Illustrator trade-dress run (issue #, colourway, variant ratio, price, barcode zone, per row) and renders a 4-colour Retro spot-separation/registration sheet, all at comic trim+bleed (6.625x10.25in, 0.125in bleed, 300dpi).
tasks.mcp_workflow: asset_initialize_file_upload -> asset_finalize_file_upload -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_crop_and_resize -> image_select_subject -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> image_invert_selection -> image_adjust_color_temperature -> image_adjust_dark_portions -> image_apply_halftone -> image_adjust_hsl -> image_apply_monochromatic_tint -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast -> image_apply_glitch_effect -> image_add_grain -> asset_search -> asset_license_and_download_stock -> image_apply_color_overlay -> image_add_noise -> image_vectorize -> asset_initialize_file_upload -> asset_finalize_file_upload -> image_vectorize -> font_recommend -> image_apply_halftone -> document_merge_data_vector [T] -> document_render_vector -> document_render_vector
